revision.comment: 40 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:多治比縣守 up to rev 100888393
revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 多治比縣守 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[奈良時代]] | 生誕 = [[天智天皇]]7年（[[668年]]） | 死没 = [[天平]]9年[[6月23日 (旧暦)|6月23日]]（[[737年]][[7月25日]]） | 改名 = | 別名 = | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = [[正三位]]・[[中納言]] | 主君 = [[文武天皇]]→[[元明天皇]]→[[元正天皇]]→[[聖武天皇]] | 氏族 = [[多治比氏|多治比]][[真人]] | 父母 = 父：[[多治比嶋]] | 兄弟 = [[多治比池守|池守]]、[[多治比水守|水守]]、'''縣守'''、[[多治比広成|広成]]、[[多治比広足|広足]]、阿伎良 | 妻 = | 子 = [[藤原浜成]]室、[[丹比郎女]]? | 特記事項 = }}
'''多治比 縣守'''（たじひ の あがたもり）は、[[奈良時代]]の[[公卿]]。[[左大臣]]・[[多治比嶋]]の子。
[[養老]]の[[遣唐使]]（[[717年]]-[[718年]]）で遣唐[[押使]]として渡唐を果たしたのち、[[按察使 (日本)|按察使]]・[[征夷大将軍|征夷将軍]]などを歴任して地方行政に従事する。 [[神亀]]6年（[[729年]]）の[[長屋王#長屋王の変|長屋王の変]]に際して臨時の[[参議]]になると、[[藤原四兄弟|藤原四子政権]]で昇進し[[正三位]]・[[中納言]]に至った。
== 経歴 == 生年が明記された史料はないが、『[[公卿補任]]』にある「''天平9年''（737年） ''薨、年七十''」との記述に拠れば[[天智天皇]]7年（[[668年]]）生まれとなる<ref name=naoki>直木[2006: 617]</ref>。[[多治比氏]]は[[宣化天皇]]を出自にもち、父・嶋は左大臣を務めるなど、当時の名門家であった<ref>高島[1969 10-11]</ref>。
=== 遣唐押使として渡唐 === [[慶雲]]2年（[[705年]]）[[従六位|従六位上]]から三階昇進して[[従五位|従五位下]]に[[叙爵]]する。[[元明天皇|元明]]朝では、[[和銅]]4年（[[711年]]）従五位上、和銅8年（[[715年]]）正月には一挙に三階昇進して[[従四位|従四位下]]と順調に昇進し、同年5月には[[造宮省|造宮卿]]に任ぜられ[[平城宮]]の造営を担当する。
[[元正天皇|元正]]朝に入り、[[霊亀]]2年（[[716年]]）8月に[[遣唐使|遣唐押使]]に任命され、翌霊亀3年（[[717年]]）3月に[[節刀]]を授けられる<ref>『続日本紀』霊亀3年3月9日条</ref>。渡唐行程についての詳細な記録はないが、おそらく同年6月～7月ごろに出発して、南路から[[長江]]河口部または[[杭州]]に着いたものと推定されている<ref name=ueda83>上田[2006: 83]</ref>。[[長安]]に到着したのは同年の10月1日であった<ref>上田[2006 80-81]</ref>。このときの遣唐使一行には、留学生に[[阿倍仲麻呂]]・[[吉備真備]]・[[井真成]]らが、留学僧に[[玄昉]]などがおり<ref>上田[2006: 79]</ref>、また使節団の要員も前回（[[大宝 (日本)|大宝]]2年〔[[702年]]〕）の倍以上である557人（船4隻）という大規模なものであった。[[養老]]2年（[[718年]]）10月に使節団は一人の犠牲者も出さずに無事帰国した<ref>『続日本紀』養老2年10月20日条</ref>。同年12月に県守は復命を果たし、翌養老3年（[[719年]]）正月には二階の昇叙を受けて[[正四位|正四位下]]となる。
その後[[武蔵国司#武蔵守|武蔵国守]]に任ぜられると、同年7月には新設された[[按察使 (日本)|按察使]]の一人として、[[相模国]]・[[上野国]]及び[[下野国]]を管轄する。まもなく[[播磨国]]按察使に転じる。
=== 蝦夷征討 === 養老4年（[[720年]]）9月に[[陸奥国]]按察使（[[陸奥按察使]]）・[[上毛野広人]]が殺害され<ref>『続日本紀』養老4年9月28日条</ref>、史上初の大規模な[[蝦夷]]による反乱が発生した<ref>鈴木[2008: 44]</ref>。当時の朝廷は、[[藤原不比等]]が8月に没して[[長屋王]]が政権を握ったばかりであったが、乱の報を受け迅速に対応する。翌日の9月29日には縣守は遣唐使使節団を率いた統率力と、東国の地方官（武蔵国守）を務めた経験を買われ<ref name=suzuki4546>鈴木[2008: 45-46]</ref>、二度目の節刀を授けられて[[征夷大将軍|持節征夷将軍]]に任じられ、副将軍に任じられた[[下毛野石代]]、[[鎮狄将軍|持節鎮狄将軍]]・[[阿倍駿河]]と共に反乱鎮圧のために[[東北地方]]へ遠征する<ref>[[#森田 (1993)|森田 (1993)]],p.164</ref>。
按察使・上毛野広人殺害の状況を含むこの蝦夷の反乱の経緯は明らかでない。一方で、[[宮城県]][[大崎市]]にある[[官衙]]遺跡の権現山遺跡・三輪田遺跡と南小林遺跡が火災によって廃絶していることが調査から明らかとなっており、反乱はこの大崎地方で起こり遺跡の焼失が発生したものと考えられる<ref>鈴木[2008: 45]</ref>。反乱鎮圧はある程度の戦果を挙げたらしく、養老5年（[[721年]]）正月に県守は正四位上に昇叙され、4月には駿河と共に平城京へ帰還を果たした。同年6月[[中務省|中務卿]]に任ぜられて京官に復す。
=== 長屋王の変 === [[神亀]]6年（[[729年]]）2月に発生した長屋王の変に際して、[[弁官|左大弁]]・[[石川石足]]や[[弾正尹]]・[[大伴道足]]と共に臨時の[[参議]]に任ぜられ、乱後の3月に行われた[[叙位]]において[[従三位]]に叙せられ公卿に列す。その後も天平4年（[[732年]]）[[中納言]]、天平6年（[[734年]]）[[正三位]]と[[藤原四兄弟|藤原四子]]政権下で順調に昇進する。また、同年8月に節度使が設置されると[[山陰道]][[節度使 (日本)|節度使]]に任じられて、[[因幡国]]・[[伯耆国]]・[[出雲国]]・[[石見国]]・[[安芸国]]・[[周防国]]・[[長門国]]の[[警固|警固式]]（対外防衛マニュアル）を策定している<ref>『続日本紀』天応元年4月1日条</ref>。なおこの時に、先の遣唐使で副使として共に渡唐した[[藤原宇合]]も[[西海道]]節度使に任ぜられており、この節度使制度はこの遣唐使を通じて[[唐]]から伝わったとする意見もある<ref>大原良通「唐の節度使と日本の遣唐使」『史泉』第77号、1993年</ref>。
天平7年（[[735年]]）入京した[[新羅使]]・金相貞に対して遣使の趣旨確認を行うが、[[国号]]を「王城国」に改称したと告知したため、無断で国号を改めたことに対して無礼と責め使者を追い返している<ref>『続日本紀』天平7年2月27日条</ref>。
天平9年（[[737年]]）6月23日薨去。享年70<ref>『公卿補任』</ref>。最終[[官位]]は中納言正三位。当時大流行して[[藤原四兄弟]]も命を落とした[[天然痘]]を死因とする説もある<ref name=naoki/>。
== 官歴 == 『[[続日本紀]]』による。 *時期不詳：[[従六位|従六位上]] *[[慶雲]]2年（[[705年]]） 12月27日：[[従五位|従五位下]] *[[和銅]]4年（[[711年]]） 4月7日：従五位上 *和銅8年（[[715年]]） 正月10日：[[従四位|従四位下]]（越階）。5月22日：[[造宮省|造宮卿]] *霊亀2年（[[716年]]） 8月20日：[[遣唐使|遣唐押使]] *[[養老]]2年（[[718年]]） 10月：帰国 *養老3年（[[719年]]） 正月13日：[[正四位|正四位下]]（越階）。日付不詳：[[武蔵国司#武蔵守|武蔵国守]]。7月13日：[[相模国|相模]][[上野国|上野]][[下野国|下野]][[按察使 (日本)|按察使]] *時期不詳：[[播磨国|播磨]]按察使 *養老4年（[[720年]]） 9月29日：[[征夷大将軍|持節征夷将軍]] *養老5年（[[721年]]） 正月5日：正四位上。6月26日：[[中務省|中務卿]] *[[神亀]]6年（[[729年]]） 2月11日：[[参議|権参議]]。[[民部省|民部卿]]。3月4日：[[従三位]] *天平3年（[[731年]]） 8月11日：参議。11月22日：[[山陽道]][[鎮撫使 (古代日本)|鎮撫使]] *天平4年（[[732年]]） 正月20日：[[中納言]]。8月17日：[[山陰道]][[節度使 (日本)|節度使]] *天平6年（[[734年]]） 正月17日：[[正三位]] *天平9年（[[737年]]） 6月23日：薨去（中納言正三位）
== 系譜 == *父：[[多治比嶋]]<ref>『続日本紀』天平9年6月23日条</ref> *母：不詳 *生母不詳の子女 **女子：[[藤原浜成]]室<ref>『尊卑分脈』</ref> **女子：[[丹比郎女]]<ref>川上富吉「「贈京丹比家歌」二首考--家持の係累をめぐって」『大妻国文』1971-03</ref> - [[大伴旅人]]室、[[大伴家持]]母?
== 脚注 == {{Reflist|2}}
== 出典 == *[[宇治谷孟]]『続日本紀 （上）』[[講談社]]〈[[講談社学術文庫]]〉、1992年 *{{Cite Journal|和書|author=直木孝次郎|authorlink=直木孝次郎 |year=2006 |title=多治比県守 |journal=日本古代中世人名辞典 |publisher=[[吉川弘文館]] |page=617 |isbn=4-642-01434-9|ref=直木 (2006)}} *{{Cite journal|和書|author=森公章|authorlink=森公章 |year=1994 |title=多治比県守|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%A4%9A%E6%B2%BB%E6%AF%94%E7%9C%8C%E5%AE%88-93252 |journal=朝日日本歴史人物事典 |publisher=[[朝日新聞社]] |page=1015 |isbn=4-02-340052-1 |ref=森 (1994)}} *{{Cite book|和書|author=鈴木拓也|authorlink=鈴木拓也 |year=2008 |title=蝦夷と東北戦争 |series=戦争の日本史 3 |publisher=[[吉川弘文館]] |isbn=978-4642063135 |ref=鈴木 (2008)}} *{{Cite book|和書|author=上田雄|authorlink=上田雄 |year=2006 |title=遣唐使全航海 |publisher=[[草思社]] |isbn=978-4794215444 |ref=上田 (2006)}} *{{Cite journal|和書|author=[[森田悌]] |title=長屋王の治政 : 初期律令国家理解への一視角（六）|url= |journal=教科教育研究 |publisher=[[金沢大学]] |date=1993-07 |year=1993 |volume=25 |issue= |pages= |hdl=2297/23323 |crid=1050282810904004352 |issn=0287-1122 |ref=森田 (1993)}} *{{Cite journal|和書|author=[[高島正人]] |title=奈良時代における公卿補任の性格 |url= |journal=立正大学人文科学研究所年報 |publisher=[[立正大学]] |date=1969-12 |year=1969 |volume=7 |issue= |pages=3-26 |hdl=11266/1389 |crid=1520853835308255232 |issn=0389-9535 |ref=高島 (1969)}} * [[宝賀寿男]] 編著『古代氏族系譜集成』上中下、古代氏族研究会、1986年。{{国立国会図書館書誌ID|000001831027}}。 * 中田憲信 編、木村信行 訳『皇胤志 第二巻』日本歴史研究所、1994年。{{国立国会図書館書誌ID|000002407591}}。
{{遣隋・遣唐大使}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たしひ の あかたもり}} [[Category:多治比氏|あかたもり]] [[Category:奈良時代の貴族]] [[Category:遣唐使]] [[Category:668年生]] [[Category:737年没]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[多治比縣守]] to [[Tajihi no Agatamori]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 多治比縣守 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[奈良時代]] | 生誕 = [[天智天皇]]7年（[[668年]]） | 死没 = [[天平]]9年[[6月23日 (旧暦)|6月23日]]（[[737年]][[7月25日]]） | 改名 = | 別名 = | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = [[正三位]]・[[中納言]] | 主君 = [[文武天皇]]→[[元明天皇]]→[[元正天皇]]→[[聖武天皇]] | 氏族 = [[多治比氏|多治比]][[真人]] | 父母 = 父：[[多治比嶋]] | 兄弟 = [[多治比池守|池守]]、[[多治比水守|水守]]、'''縣守'''、[[多治比広成|広成]]、[[多治比広足|広足]]、阿伎良 | 妻 = | 子 = [[藤原浜成]]室、[[丹比郎女]]? | 特記事項 = }}
'''多治比 縣守'''（たじひ の あがたもり）は、[[奈良時代]]の[[公卿]]。[[左大臣]]・[[多治比嶋]]の子。
[[養老]]の[[遣唐使]]（[[717年]]-[[718年]]）で遣唐[[押使]]として渡唐を果たしたのち、[[按察使 (日本)|按察使]]・[[征夷大将軍|征夷将軍]]などを歴任して地方行政に従事する。 [[神亀]]6年（[[729年]]）の[[長屋王#長屋王の変|長屋王の変]]に際して臨時の[[参議]]になると、[[藤原四兄弟|藤原四子政権]]で昇進し[[正三位]]・[[中納言]]に至った。
== 経歴 == 生年が明記された史料はないが、『[[公卿補任]]』にある「''天平9年''（737年） ''薨、年七十''」との記述に拠れば[[天智天皇]]7年（[[668年]]）生まれとなる<ref name=naoki>直木[2006: 617]</ref>。[[多治比氏]]は[[宣化天皇]]を出自にもち、父・嶋は左大臣を務めるなど、当時の名門家であった<ref>高島[1969 10-11]</ref>。
=== 遣唐押使として渡唐 === [[慶雲]]2年（[[705年]]）[[従六位|従六位上]]から三階昇進して[[従五位|従五位下]]に[[叙爵]]する。[[元明天皇|元明]]朝では、[[和銅]]4年（[[711年]]）従五位上、和銅8年（[[715年]]）正月には一挙に三階昇進して[[従四位|従四位下]]と順調に昇進し、同年5月には[[造宮省|造宮卿]]に任ぜられ[[平城宮]]の造営を担当する。
[[元正天皇|元正]]朝に入り、[[霊亀]]2年（[[716年]]）8月に[[遣唐使|遣唐押使]]に任命され、翌霊亀3年（[[717年]]）3月に[[節刀]]を授けられる<ref>『続日本紀』霊亀3年3月9日条</ref>。渡唐行程についての詳細な記録はないが、おそらく同年6月～7月ごろに出発して、南路から[[長江]]河口部または[[杭州]]に着いたものと推定されている<ref name=ueda83>上田[2006: 83]</ref>。[[長安]]に到着したのは同年の10月1日であった<ref>上田[2006 80-81]</ref>。このときの遣唐使一行には、留学生に[[阿倍仲麻呂]]・[[吉備真備]]・[[井真成]]らが、留学僧に[[玄昉]]などがおり<ref>上田[2006: 79]</ref>、また使節団の要員も前回（[[大宝 (日本)|大宝]]2年〔[[702年]]〕）の倍以上である557人（船4隻）という大規模なものであった。[[養老]]2年（[[718年]]）10月に使節団は一人の犠牲者も出さずに無事帰国した<ref>『続日本紀』養老2年10月20日条</ref>。同年12月に県守は復命を果たし、翌養老3年（[[719年]]）正月には二階の昇叙を受けて[[正四位|正四位下]]となる。
その後[[武蔵国司#武蔵守|武蔵国守]]に任ぜられると、同年7月には新設された[[按察使 (日本)|按察使]]の一人として、[[相模国]]・[[上野国]]及び[[下野国]]を管轄する。まもなく[[播磨国]]按察使に転じる。
=== 蝦夷征討 === 養老4年（[[720年]]）9月に[[陸奥国]]按察使（[[陸奥按察使]]）・[[上毛野広人]]が殺害され<ref>『続日本紀』養老4年9月28日条</ref>、史上初の大規模な[[蝦夷]]による反乱が発生した<ref>鈴木[2008: 44]</ref>。当時の朝廷は、[[藤原不比等]]が8月に没して[[長屋王]]が政権を握ったばかりであったが、乱の報を受け迅速に対応する。翌日の9月29日には縣守は遣唐使使節団を率いた統率力と、東国の地方官（武蔵国守）を務めた経験を買われ<ref name=suzuki4546>鈴木[2008: 45-46]</ref>、二度目の節刀を授けられて[[征夷大将軍|持節征夷将軍]]に任じられ、副将軍に任じられた[[下毛野石代]]、[[鎮狄将軍|持節鎮狄将軍]]・[[阿倍駿河]]と共に反乱鎮圧のために[[東北地方]]へ遠征する<ref>[[#森田 (1993)|森田 (1993)]],p.164</ref>。
按察使・上毛野広人殺害の状況を含むこの蝦夷の反乱の経緯は明らかでない。一方で、[[宮城県]][[大崎市]]にある[[官衙]]遺跡の権現山遺跡・三輪田遺跡と南小林遺跡が火災によって廃絶していることが調査から明らかとなっており、反乱はこの大崎地方で起こり遺跡の焼失が発生したものと考えられる<ref>鈴木[2008: 45]</ref>。反乱鎮圧はある程度の戦果を挙げたらしく、養老5年（[[721年]]）正月に県守は正四位上に昇叙され、4月には駿河と共に平城京へ帰還を果たした。同年6月[[中務省|中務卿]]に任ぜられて京官に復す。
=== 長屋王の変 === [[神亀]]6年（[[729年]]）2月に発生した長屋王の変に際して、[[弁官|左大弁]]・[[石川石足]]や[[弾正尹]]・[[大伴道足]]と共に臨時の[[参議]]に任ぜられ、乱後の3月に行われた[[叙位]]において[[従三位]]に叙せられ公卿に列す。その後も天平4年（[[732年]]）[[中納言]]、天平6年（[[734年]]）[[正三位]]と[[藤原四兄弟|藤原四子]]政権下で順調に昇進する。また、同年8月に節度使が設置されると[[山陰道]][[節度使 (日本)|節度使]]に任じられて、[[因幡国]]・[[伯耆国]]・[[出雲国]]・[[石見国]]・[[安芸国]]・[[周防国]]・[[長門国]]の[[警固|警固式]]（対外防衛マニュアル）を策定している<ref>『続日本紀』天応元年4月1日条</ref>。なおこの時に、先の遣唐使で副使として共に渡唐した[[藤原宇合]]も[[西海道]]節度使に任ぜられており、この節度使制度はこの遣唐使を通じて[[唐]]から伝わったとする意見もある<ref>大原良通「唐の節度使と日本の遣唐使」『史泉』第77号、1993年</ref>。
天平7年（[[735年]]）入京した[[新羅使]]・金相貞に対して遣使の趣旨確認を行うが、[[国号]]を「王城国」に改称したと告知したため、無断で国号を改めたことに対して無礼と責め使者を追い返している<ref>『続日本紀』天平7年2月27日条</ref>。
天平9年（[[737年]]）6月23日薨去。享年70<ref>『公卿補任』</ref>。最終[[官位]]は中納言正三位。当時大流行して[[藤原四兄弟]]も命を落とした[[天然痘]]を死因とする説もある<ref name=naoki/>。
== 官歴 == 『[[続日本紀]]』による。 *時期不詳：[[従六位|従六位上]] *[[慶雲]]2年（[[705年]]） 12月27日：[[従五位|従五位下]] *[[和銅]]4年（[[711年]]） 4月7日：従五位上 *和銅8年（[[715年]]） 正月10日：[[従四位|従四位下]]（越階）。5月22日：[[造宮省|造宮卿]] *霊亀2年（[[716年]]） 8月20日：[[遣唐使|遣唐押使]] *[[養老]]2年（[[718年]]） 10月：帰国 *養老3年（[[719年]]） 正月13日：[[正四位|正四位下]]（越階）。日付不詳：[[武蔵国司#武蔵守|武蔵国守]]。7月13日：[[相模国|相模]][[上野国|上野]][[下野国|下野]][[按察使 (日本)|按察使]] *時期不詳：[[播磨国|播磨]]按察使 *養老4年（[[720年]]） 9月29日：[[征夷大将軍|持節征夷将軍]] *養老5年（[[721年]]） 正月5日：正四位上。6月26日：[[中務省|中務卿]] *[[神亀]]6年（[[729年]]） 2月11日：[[参議|権参議]]。[[民部省|民部卿]]。3月4日：[[従三位]] *天平3年（[[731年]]） 8月11日：参議。11月22日：[[山陽道]][[鎮撫使 (古代日本)|鎮撫使]] *天平4年（[[732年]]） 正月20日：[[中納言]]。8月17日：[[山陰道]][[節度使 (日本)|節度使]] *天平6年（[[734年]]） 正月17日：[[正三位]] *天平9年（[[737年]]） 6月23日：薨去（中納言正三位）
== 系譜 == *父：[[多治比嶋]]<ref>『続日本紀』天平9年6月23日条</ref> *母：不詳 *生母不詳の子女 **女子：[[藤原浜成]]室<ref>『尊卑分脈』</ref> **女子：[[丹比郎女]]<ref>川上富吉「「贈京丹比家歌」二首考--家持の係累をめぐって」『大妻国文』1971-03</ref> - [[大伴旅人]]室、[[大伴家持]]母?
== 脚注 == {{Reflist|2}}
== 出典 == *[[宇治谷孟]]『続日本紀 （上）』[[講談社]]〈[[講談社学術文庫]]〉、1992年 *{{Cite Journal|和書|author=直木孝次郎|authorlink=直木孝次郎 |year=2006 |title=多治比県守 |journal=日本古代中世人名辞典 |publisher=[[吉川弘文館]] |page=617 |isbn=4-642-01434-9|ref=直木 (2006)}} *{{Cite journal|和書|author=森公章|authorlink=森公章 |year=1994 |title=多治比県守|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%A4%9A%E6%B2%BB%E6%AF%94%E7%9C%8C%E5%AE%88-93252 |journal=朝日日本歴史人物事典 |publisher=[[朝日新聞社]] |page=1015 |isbn=4-02-340052-1 |ref=森 (1994)}} *{{Cite book|和書|author=鈴木拓也|authorlink=鈴木拓也 |year=2008 |title=蝦夷と東北戦争 |series=戦争の日本史 3 |publisher=[[吉川弘文館]] |isbn=978-4642063135 |ref=鈴木 (2008)}} *{{Cite book|和書|author=上田雄|authorlink=上田雄 |year=2006 |title=遣唐使全航海 |publisher=[[草思社]] |isbn=978-4794215444 |ref=上田 (2006)}} *{{Cite journal|和書|author=[[森田悌]] |title=長屋王の治政 : 初期律令国家理解への一視角（六）|url= |journal=教科教育研究 |publisher=[[金沢大学]] |date=1993-07 |year=1993 |volume=25 |issue= |pages= |hdl=2297/23323 |crid=1050282810904004352 |issn=0287-1122 |ref=森田 (1993)}} *{{Cite journal|和書|author=[[高島正人]] |title=奈良時代における公卿補任の性格 |url= |journal=立正大学人文科学研究所年報 |publisher=[[立正大学]] |date=1969-12 |year=1969 |volume=7 |issue= |pages=3-26 |hdl=11266/1389 |crid=1520853835308255232 |issn=0389-9535 |ref=高島 (1969)}} * [[宝賀寿男]] 編著『古代氏族系譜集成』上中下、古代氏族研究会、1986年。{{国立国会図書館書誌ID|000001831027}}。 * 中田憲信 編、木村信行 訳『皇胤志 第二巻』日本歴史研究所、1994年。{{国立国会図書館書誌ID|000002407591}}。
{{遣隋・遣唐大使}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たしひ の あかたもり}} [[Category:多治比氏|あかたもり]] [[Category:奈良時代の貴族]] [[Category:遣唐使]] [[Category:668年生]] [[Category:737年没]]

'''Tajihi no Agatamori''' (多治比縣守) was a court noble and government official during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. He was a son of {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who served as a {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Minister of the Left). Agatamori played key roles in diplomacy, governance, and military affairs, including serving as {{ill|Envoy to Tang China|ja|遣唐使押使|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Governor of Musashi|ja|武蔵守|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Seii Taishōgun|ja|征夷大将軍|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Biography ==
Agatamori was born in the 7th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s reign (668), as recorded in sources like the {{ill|Kugyō Bōnin|ja|Kugyō補任|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which states he passed away in {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (737) at the age of 70.<ref name="naoki">Naoki [2006: 617]</ref> His family, the {{ill|Tajihi clan|ja|多治比氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, descended from {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, making them one of the prominent noble lineages of the time.<ref>Takashima [1969: 10–11]</ref>
=== Mission to Tang China ===
In {{ill|Keiun 2|ja|慶雲2nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (705), Agatamori was promoted from {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. His career advanced rapidly under the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and by {{ill|Wadō 8|ja|和銅8th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (715), he had reached {{ill|Jūshi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and was appointed {{ill|Zōkyūkyō|ja|造宮卿|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, overseeing the construction of {{ill|Heijō Palace|ja|平城宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
In {{ill|Reiki 2|ja|霊亀2nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (716), Agatamori was appointed Envoy to Tang China (遣唐押使). He departed for China in {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (717), leading a large delegation of 557 members, including notable figures such as {{ill|Abe no Nakamaro|ja|阿倍仲麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Gembō|ja|玄昉|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The delegation reached {{ill|Chang'an|ja|長安|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in October and returned safely in {{ill|Yōrō 2|ja|養老2nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (718). Agatamori received further promotions upon his return.<ref>Ueda [2006: 80–83]</ref>
=== Governor and Regional Commander ===
Agatamori was later appointed {{ill|Governor of Musashi|ja|武蔵守|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and subsequently became one of the first {{ill|Inspectors (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, overseeing the provinces of {{ill|Sagami|ja|相模|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kōzuke|ja|上野|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. He also served as {{ill|Governor of Harima|ja|播磨守|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (720), he was appointed {{ill|Seii Taishōgun|ja|征夷大将軍|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to suppress a rebellion by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, following the assassination of {{ill|Kamu Yamakano|ja|上毛野広人|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the Inspector of Mutsu Province.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45–46]</ref> His campaign achieved partial success, and he was promoted to {{ill|Jūshi-i|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft}} upon his return.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 44]</ref>
=== Role in Court Politics ===
In {{ill|Shinki 6|ja|神亀6年|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (729), Agatamori was appointed as a provisional {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during the {{ill|Conspiracy of Prince Nagaya|ja|長屋王の変|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and became a court noble shortly afterward, holding key positions under the {{ill|Fujiwara Four Brothers|ja|藤原四兄弟|qq=draft|qq=draft}} regime. He reached the Rank of {{ill|Shōsan-i|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and served as {{ill|Chūnagon|ja|中納言|1=Chūnagon|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
In {{ill|Tenpyō 7|ja|天平7th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (735), he rejected a delegation from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} that had unilaterally changed its national name.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Tenpyō 7, 2/27</ref>
Agatamori died in {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (737) at age 70, likely due to the smallpox epidemic that claimed many lives, including the Fujiwara brothers.<ref name="naoki" />
== Family ==
* Father: {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Tenpyō 9, 6/23</ref> * Children: ** Daughter: Wife of {{ill|Fujiwara no Hamanari|ja|藤原浜成|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref>‘Sonpi Bunmyaku’</ref> ** Daughter: Possibly {{ill|Tanbe no Iratsume|ja|丹比郎女|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, wife of {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|ja|大伴旅人|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and mother of {{ill|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|ja|大伴家持|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref>Kawakami [1971]</ref>
== References ==
{{Reflist|2}}

In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (720), he was appointed {{ill|Seii Taishōgun|ja|征夷大将軍|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to suppress a rebellion by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, following the assassination of {{ill|Kamu Yamakano|ja|上毛野広人|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the Inspector of Mutsu Province.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45–46]</ref> His campaign achieved partial success, and he was promoted to {{ill|Jūshi-i|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft}} upon his return.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 44]</ref>

In {{ill|Shinki 6|ja|神亀6年|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (729), Agatamori was appointed as a provisional {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during the {{ill|Conspiracy of Prince Nagaya|ja|長屋王の変|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and became a court noble shortly afterward, holding key positions under the {{ill|Fujiwara Four Brothers|ja|藤原四兄弟|qq=draft|qq=draft}} regime. He reached the Rank of {{ill|Shōsan-i|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and served as {{ill|Chūnagon|ja|中納言|1=Chūnagon|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
In {{ill|Tenpyō 7|ja|天平7th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (735), he rejected a delegation from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} that had unilaterally changed its national name.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Tenpyō 7, 2/27</ref>
Agatamori died in {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (737) at age 70, likely due to the smallpox epidemic that claimed many lives, including the Fujiwara brothers.<ref name="naoki" />
== Family ==
* Father: {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Tenpyō 9, 6/23</ref> * Children: ** Daughter: Wife of {{ill|Fujiwara no Hamanari|ja|藤原浜成|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref>‘Sonpi Bunmyaku’</ref> ** Daughter: Possibly {{ill|Tanbe no Iratsume|ja|丹比郎女|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, wife of {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|ja|大伴旅人|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and mother of {{ill|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|ja|大伴家持|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref>Kawakami [1971]</ref>
== References ==
{{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography ==
* Ueda, Yū. *{{ill|遣唐使全航海|ja}}*. Sōshisha, 2006. ISBN 978-4794215444. * Suzuki, Takuya. *{{ill|Emishi to Tōhoku Sensō|ja|蝦夷と東北戦争|qq=draft|qq=draft}}*. Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2008. ISBN 978-4642063135. * Naoki, Kōjirō. *Nihon Kodai Chūsei Jinmei Jiten*. Yoshikawa Kōbu
{{translated page|ja|多治比縣守|version=100888393|comment=Imported full ja history}}

[[sn:Tajihi no Agatamori]] [[ja:多治比縣守]] [[en:Tajihi no Agatamori]]
<!--Tajihi no Agatamori--><!--Tajihi no Agatamori--><!--Tajihi no Agatamori--><!--Tajihi no Agatamori-->

'''Tajihi no Agatamori''' (多治比縣守) was a court noble and government official during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. He was a son of {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who served as a {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Minister of the Left). Agatamori played key roles in diplomacy, governance, and military affairs, including serving as {{ill|Envoy to Tang China|ja|遣唐使押使|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Governor of Musashi|ja|武蔵守|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Seii Taishōgun|ja|征夷大将軍|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Biography ==
Agatamori was born in the 7th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s reign (668), as recorded in sources like the {{ill|Kugyō Bōnin|ja|Kugyō補任|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which states he passed away in {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (737) at the age of 70.<ref name="naoki">Naoki [2006: 617]</ref> His family, the {{ill|Tajihi clan|ja|多治比氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, descended from {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, making them one of the prominent noble lineages of the time.<ref>Takashima [1969: 10–11]</ref>
=== Mission to Tang China ===
In {{ill|Keiun 2|ja|慶雲2nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (705), Agatamori was promoted from {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. His career advanced rapidly under the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and by {{ill|Wadō 8|ja|和銅8th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (715), he had reached {{ill|Jūshi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and was appointed {{ill|Zōkyūkyō|ja|造宮卿|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, overseeing the construction of {{ill|Heijō Palace|ja|平城宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
In {{ill|Reiki 2|ja|霊亀2nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (716), Agatamori was appointed Envoy to Tang China (遣唐押使). He departed for China in {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (717), leading a large delegation of 557 members, including notable figures such as {{ill|Abe no Nakamaro|ja|阿倍仲麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Gembō|ja|玄昉|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The delegation reached {{ill|Chang'an|ja|長安|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in October and returned safely in {{ill|Yōrō 2|ja|養老2nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (718). Agatamori received further promotions upon his return.<ref>Ueda [2006: 80–83]</ref>
=== Governor and Regional Commander ===
Agatamori was later appointed {{ill|Governor of Musashi|ja|武蔵守|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and subsequently became one of the first {{ill|Inspectors (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, overseeing the provinces of {{ill|Sagami|ja|相模|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kōzuke|ja|上野|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. He also served as {{ill|Governor of Harima|ja|播磨守|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (720), he was appointed {{ill|Seii Taishōgun|ja|征夷大将軍|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to suppress a rebellion by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, following the assassination of {{ill|Kamu Yamakano|ja|上毛野広人|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the Inspector of Mutsu Province.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45–46]</ref> His campaign achieved partial success, and he was promoted to {{ill|Jūshi-i|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft}} upon his return.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 44]</ref>
=== Role in Court Politics ===
In {{ill|Shinki 6|ja|神亀6年|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (729), Agatamori was appointed as a provisional {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during the {{ill|Conspiracy of Prince Nagaya|ja|長屋王の変|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and became a court noble shortly afterward, holding key positions under the {{ill|Fujiwara Four Brothers|ja|藤原四兄弟|qq=draft|qq=draft}} regime. He reached the Rank of {{ill|Shōsan-i|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and served as {{ill|Chūnagon|ja|中納言|1=Chūnagon|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
In {{ill|Tenpyō 7|ja|天平7th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (735), he rejected a delegation from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} that had unilaterally changed its national name.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Tenpyō 7, 2/27</ref>
Agatamori died in {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (737) at age 70, likely due to the smallpox epidemic that claimed many lives, including the Fujiwara brothers.<ref name="naoki" />
== Family ==
* Father: {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Tenpyō 9, 6/23</ref> * Children: ** Daughter: Wife of {{ill|Fujiwara no Hamanari|ja|藤原浜成|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref>‘Sonpi Bunmyaku’</ref> ** Daughter: Possibly {{ill|Tanbe no Iratsume|ja|丹比郎女|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, wife of {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|ja|大伴旅人|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and mother of {{ill|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|ja|大伴家持|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref>Kawakami [1971]</ref>
== References ==
{{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography ==
* Ueda, Yū. *{{ill|遣唐使全航海|ja}}*. Sōshisha, 2006. ISBN 978-4794215444. * Suzuki, Takuya. *{{ill|Emishi to Tōhoku Sensō|ja|蝦夷と東北戦争|qq=draft|qq=draft}}*. Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2008. ISBN 978-4642063135. * Naoki, Kōjirō. *Nihon Kodai Chūsei Jinmei Jiten*. Yoshikawa Kōbu

His career advanced rapidly under the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Genmei|qq=}}, and by {{ill|Wadō 8|ja|和銅8th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 8|qq=}} (715), he had reached {{ill|Jūshi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} and was appointed {{ill|Zōkyūkyō|ja|造宮卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōkyūkyō|qq=}}, overseeing the construction of {{ill|Heijō Palace|ja|平城宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heijō Palace|qq=}}.
In {{ill|Reiki 2|ja|霊亀2nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 2|qq=}} (716), Agatamori was appointed Envoy to Tang China (遣唐押使). He departed for China in {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 3|qq=}} (717), leading a large delegation of 557 members, including notable figures such as {{ill|Abe no Nakamaro|ja|阿倍仲麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Nakamaro|qq=}}, {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi no Makibi|qq=}}, and {{ill|Gembō|ja|玄昉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gembō|qq=}}. The delegation reached {{ill|Chang'an|ja|長安|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chang'an|qq=}} in October and returned safely in {{ill|Yōrō 2|ja|養老2nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 2|qq=}} (718). Agatamori received further promotions upon his return.<ref>Ueda [2006: 80–83]</ref>
=== Governor and Regional Commander ===
Agatamori was later appointed {{ill|Governor of Musashi|ja|武蔵守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Musashi|qq=}} and subsequently became one of the first {{ill|Inspectors (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inspectors (Ancient Japan)|qq=}}, overseeing the provinces of {{ill|Sagami|ja|相模|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagami|qq=}}, {{ill|Kōzuke|ja|上野|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōzuke|qq=}}, and {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke|qq=}}. He also served as {{ill|Governor of Harima|ja|播磨守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Harima|qq=}}.
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 4|qq=}} (720), he was appointed {{ill|Seii Taishōgun|ja|征夷大将軍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seii Taishōgun|qq=}} to suppress a rebellion by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi|qq=}}, following the assassination of {{ill|Kamu Yamakano|ja|上毛野広人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamu Yamakano|qq=}}, the Inspector of Mutsu Province.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45–46]</ref> His campaign achieved partial success, and he was promoted to {{ill|Jūshi-i|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} upon his return.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 44]</ref>
=== Role in Court Politics ===
In {{ill|Shinki 6|ja|神亀6年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinki 6|qq=}} (729), Agatamori was appointed as a provisional {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議|1=Sangi (Japan)|lt=Sangi|2=ja|3=参議|4=ru|5=Санги|6=fr|7=Sangi_(Japon)|8=ko|9=참의_(일본사)|10=es|11=Sangi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangi|qq=}} during the {{ill|Conspiracy of Prince Nagaya|ja|長屋王の変|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Conspiracy of Prince Nagaya|qq=}} and became a court noble shortly afterward, holding key positions under the {{ill|Fujiwara Four Brothers|ja|藤原四兄弟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Four Brothers|qq=}} regime. He reached the Rank of {{ill|Shōsan-i|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōsan-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} and served as {{ill|Chūnagon|ja|中納言|1=Chūnagon|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūnagon|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūnagon|qq=}}.
In {{ill|Tenpyō 7|ja|天平7th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 7|qq=}} (735), he rejected a delegation from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Silla|qq=}} that had unilaterally changed its national name.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Tenpyō 7, 2/27</ref>
Agatamori died in {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|qq=}} (737) at age 70, likely due to the smallpox epidemic that claimed many lives, including the Fujiwara brothers.<ref name="naoki" />
== Family ==
* Father: {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Shima|qq=}}<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Tenpyō 9, 6/23</ref> * Children: ** Daughter: Wife of {{ill|Fujiwara no Hamanari|ja|藤原浜成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Hamanari|qq=}}<ref>‘Sonpi Bunmyaku’</ref> ** Daughter: Possibly {{ill|Tanbe no Iratsume|ja|丹比郎女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanbe no Iratsume|qq=}}, wife of {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|ja|大伴旅人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Tabito|qq=}} and mother of {{ill|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|ja|大伴家持|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Yakamochi|qq=}}<ref>Kawakami [1971]</ref>
== References ==

== Bibliography ==

ISBN 978-4794215444. * Suzuki, Takuya. *{{ill|Emishi to Tōhoku Sensō|ja|蝦夷と東北戦争|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi to Tōhoku Sensō|qq=}}*. Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2008. ISBN 978-4642063135. * Naoki, Kōjirō. *Nihon Kodai Chūsei Jinmei Jiten*. Yoshikawa Kōbu

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|左大臣|" to "|左大臣|1=Minister of the Left|"
revision.text:
'''Tajihi no Agatamori''' (多治比縣守) was a court noble and government official during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara period|qq=}}. He was a son of {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Shima|qq=}}, who served as a {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣|1=Minister of the Left|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sadaijin|qq=}} (Minister of the Left). Agatamori played key roles in diplomacy, governance, and military affairs, including serving as {{ill|Envoy to Tang China|ja|遣唐使押使|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Envoy to Tang China|qq=}}, {{ill|Governor of Musashi|ja|武蔵守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Musashi|qq=}}, and {{ill|Seii Taishōgun|ja|征夷大将軍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seii Taishōgun|qq=}}.
== Biography ==
Agatamori was born in the 7th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenji|qq=}}'s reign (668), as recorded in sources like the {{ill|Kugyō Bōnin|ja|Kugyō補任|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō Bōnin|qq=}}, which states he passed away in {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|qq=}} (737) at the age of 70.<ref name="naoki">Naoki [2006: 617]</ref> His family, the {{ill|Tajihi clan|ja|多治比氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi clan|qq=}}, descended from {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Senka|qq=}}, making them one of the prominent noble lineages of the time.<ref>Takashima [1969: 10–11]</ref>
=== Mission to Tang China ===
In {{ill|Keiun 2|ja|慶雲2nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiun 2|qq=}} (705), Agatamori was promoted from {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=}} to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}. His career advanced rapidly under the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Genmei|qq=}}, and by {{ill|Wadō 8|ja|和銅8th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 8|qq=}} (715), he had reached {{ill|Jūshi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} and was appointed {{ill|Zōkyūkyō|ja|造宮卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōkyūkyō|qq=}}, overseeing the construction of {{ill|Heijō Palace|ja|平城宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heijō Palace|qq=}}.
In {{ill|Reiki 2|ja|霊亀2nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 2|qq=}} (716), Agatamori was appointed Envoy to Tang China (遣唐押使). He departed for China in {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 3|qq=}} (717), leading a large delegation of 557 members, including notable figures such as {{ill|Abe no Nakamaro|ja|阿倍仲麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Nakamaro|qq=}}, {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi no Makibi|qq=}}, and {{ill|Gembō|ja|玄昉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gembō|qq=}}. The delegation reached {{ill|Chang'an|ja|長安|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chang'an|qq=}} in October and returned safely in {{ill|Yōrō 2|ja|養老2nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 2|qq=}} (718). Agatamori received further promotions upon his return.<ref>Ueda [2006: 80–83]</ref>
=== Governor and Regional Commander ===
Agatamori was later appointed {{ill|Governor of Musashi|ja|武蔵守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Musashi|qq=}} and subsequently became one of the first {{ill|Inspectors (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inspectors (Ancient Japan)|qq=}}, overseeing the provinces of {{ill|Sagami|ja|相模|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagami|qq=}}, {{ill|Kōzuke|ja|上野|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōzuke|qq=}}, and {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke|qq=}}. He also served as {{ill|Governor of Harima|ja|播磨守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Harima|qq=}}.
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 4|qq=}} (720), he was appointed {{ill|Seii Taishōgun|ja|征夷大将軍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seii Taishōgun|qq=}} to suppress a rebellion by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi|qq=}}, following the assassination of {{ill|Kamu Yamakano|ja|上毛野広人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamu Yamakano|qq=}}, the Inspector of Mutsu Province.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45–46]</ref> His campaign achieved partial success, and he was promoted to {{ill|Jūshi-i|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} upon his return.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 44]</ref>
=== Role in Court Politics ===
In {{ill|Shinki 6|ja|神亀6年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinki 6|qq=}} (729), Agatamori was appointed as a provisional {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議|1=Sangi (Japan)|lt=Sangi|2=ja|3=参議|4=ru|5=Санги|6=fr|7=Sangi_(Japon)|8=ko|9=참의_(일본사)|10=es|11=Sangi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangi|qq=}} during the {{ill|Conspiracy of Prince Nagaya|ja|長屋王の変|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Conspiracy of Prince Nagaya|qq=}} and became a court noble shortly afterward, holding key positions under the {{ill|Fujiwara Four Brothers|ja|藤原四兄弟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Four Brothers|qq=}} regime. He reached the Rank of {{ill|Shōsan-i|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōsan-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} and served as {{ill|Chūnagon|ja|中納言|1=Chūnagon|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūnagon|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūnagon|qq=}}.
In {{ill|Tenpyō 7|ja|天平7th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 7|qq=}} (735), he rejected a delegation from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Silla|qq=}} that had unilaterally changed its national name.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Tenpyō 7, 2/27</ref>
Agatamori died in {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|qq=}} (737) at age 70, likely due to the smallpox epidemic that claimed many lives, including the Fujiwara brothers.<ref name="naoki" />
== Family ==
* Father: {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Shima|qq=}}<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Tenpyō 9, 6/23</ref> * Children: ** Daughter: Wife of {{ill|Fujiwara no Hamanari|ja|藤原浜成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Hamanari|qq=}}<ref>‘Sonpi Bunmyaku’</ref> ** Daughter: Possibly {{ill|Tanbe no Iratsume|ja|丹比郎女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanbe no Iratsume|qq=}}, wife of {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|ja|大伴旅人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Tabito|qq=}} and mother of {{ill|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|ja|大伴家持|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Yakamochi|qq=}}<ref>Kawakami [1971]</ref>

He also served as {{ill|Governor of Harima|ja|播磨守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Harima|qq=}}.
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 4|qq=}} (720), he was appointed {{ill|Seii Taishōgun|ja|征夷大将軍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seii Taishōgun|qq=}} to suppress a rebellion by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi|qq=}}, following the assassination of {{ill|Kamu Yamakano|ja|上毛野広人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamu Yamakano|qq=}}, the Inspector of Mutsu Province.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45–46]</ref> His campaign achieved partial success, and he was promoted to {{ill|Jūshi-i|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} upon his return.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 44]</ref>
=== Role in Court Politics ===
In {{ill|Shinki 6|ja|神亀6年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinki 6|qq=}} (729), Agatamori was appointed as a provisional {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議|1=Sangi (Japan)|lt=Sangi|2=ja|3=参議|4=ru|5=Санги|6=fr|7=Sangi_(Japon)|8=ko|9=참의_(일본사)|10=es|11=Sangi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangi|qq=}} during the {{ill|Conspiracy of Prince Nagaya|ja|長屋王の変|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Conspiracy of Prince Nagaya|qq=}} and became a court noble shortly afterward, holding key positions under the {{ill|Fujiwara Four Brothers|ja|藤原四兄弟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Four Brothers|qq=}} regime. He reached the Rank of {{ill|Shōsan-i|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōsan-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} and served as {{ill|Chūnagon|ja|中納言|1=Chūnagon|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūnagon|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūnagon|qq=}}.
In {{ill|Tenpyō 7|ja|天平7th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 7|qq=}} (735), he rejected a delegation from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Silla|qq=}} that had unilaterally changed its national name.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Tenpyō 7, 2/27</ref>

'''Tajihi no Agatamori''' (多治比縣守) was a court noble and government official during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara period|qq=}}. He was a son of {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Shima|qq=}}, who served as a {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣|1=Minister of the Left|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sadaijin|qq=}} (Minister of the Left). Agatamori played key roles in diplomacy, governance, and military affairs, including serving as {{ill|Envoy to Tang China|ja|遣唐使押使|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Envoy to Tang China|qq=}}, {{ill|Governor of Musashi|ja|武蔵守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Musashi|qq=}}, and {{ill|Seii Taishōgun|ja|征夷大将軍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seii Taishōgun|qq=}}.
== Biography ==
Agatamori was born in the 7th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenji|qq=}}'s reign (668), as recorded in sources like the {{ill|Kugyō Bōnin|ja|Kugyō補任|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō Bōnin|qq=}}, which states he passed away in {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|qq=}} (737) at the age of 70.<ref name="naoki">Naoki [2006: 617]</ref> His family, the {{ill|Tajihi clan|ja|多治比氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi clan|qq=}}, descended from {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Senka|qq=}}, making them one of the prominent noble lineages of the time.<ref>Takashima [1969: 10–11]</ref>
=== Mission to Tang China ===
In {{ill|Keiun 2|ja|慶雲2nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiun 2|qq=}} (705), Agatamori was promoted from {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=}} to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}. His career advanced rapidly under the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Genmei|qq=}}, and by {{ill|Wadō 8|ja|和銅8年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 8|qq=}} (715), he had reached {{ill|Jūshi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} and was appointed {{ill|Zōkyūkyō|ja|造宮卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōkyūkyō|qq=}}, overseeing the construction of {{ill|Heijō Palace|ja|平城宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heijō Palace|qq=}}.
In {{ill|Reiki 2|ja|霊亀2nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 2|qq=}} (716), Agatamori was appointed Envoy to Tang China (遣唐押使). He departed for China in {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 3|qq=}} (717), leading a large delegation of 557 members, including notable figures such as {{ill|Abe no Nakamaro|ja|阿倍仲麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Nakamaro|qq=}}, {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi no Makibi|qq=}}, and {{ill|Gembō|ja|玄昉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gembō|qq=}}. The delegation reached {{ill|Chang'an|ja|長安|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chang'an|qq=}} in October and returned safely in {{ill|Yōrō 2|ja|養老2nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 2|qq=}} (718). Agatamori received further promotions upon his return.<ref>Ueda [2006: 80–83]</ref>
=== Governor and Regional Commander ===
Agatamori was later appointed {{ill|Governor of Musashi|ja|武蔵守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Musashi|qq=}} and subsequently became one of the first {{ill|Inspectors (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inspectors (Ancient Japan)|qq=}}, overseeing the provinces of {{ill|Sagami|ja|相模|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagami|qq=}}, {{ill|Kōzuke|ja|上野|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōzuke|qq=}}, and {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke|qq=}}. He also served as {{ill|Governor of Harima|ja|播磨守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Harima|qq=}}.
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 4|qq=}} (720), he was appointed {{ill|Seii Taishōgun|ja|征夷大将軍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seii Taishōgun|qq=}} to suppress a rebellion by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi|qq=}}, following the assassination of {{ill|Kamu Yamakano|ja|上毛野広人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamu Yamakano|qq=}}, the Inspector of Mutsu Province.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45–46]</ref> His campaign achieved partial success, and he was promoted to {{ill|Jūshi-i|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} upon his return.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 44]</ref>
=== Role in Court Politics ===
In {{ill|Shinki 6|ja|神亀6年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinki 6|qq=}} (729), Agatamori was appointed as a provisional {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議|1=Sangi (Japan)|lt=Sangi|2=ja|3=参議|4=ru|5=Санги|6=fr|7=Sangi_(Japon)|8=ko|9=참의_(일본사)|10=es|11=Sangi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangi|qq=}} during the {{ill|Conspiracy of Prince Nagaya|ja|長屋王の変|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Conspiracy of Prince Nagaya|qq=}} and became a court noble shortly afterward, holding key positions under the {{ill|Fujiwara Four Brothers|ja|藤原四兄弟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Four Brothers|qq=}} regime. He reached the Rank of {{ill|Shōsan-i|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōsan-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} and served as {{ill|Chūnagon|ja|中納言|1=Chūnagon|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūnagon|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūnagon|qq=}}.
In {{ill|Tenpyō 7|ja|天平7年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 7|qq=}} (735), he rejected a delegation from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Silla|qq=}} that had unilaterally changed its national name.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Tenpyō 7, 2/27</ref>
Agatamori died in {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|qq=}} (737) at age 70, likely due to the smallpox epidemic that claimed many lives, including the Fujiwara brothers.<ref name="naoki" />

He reached the Rank of {{ill|Shōsan-i|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōsan-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} and served as {{ill|Chūnagon|ja|中納言|1=Chūnagon|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūnagon|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūnagon|qq=}}.
In {{ill|Tenpyō 7|ja|天平7年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 7|qq=}} (735), he rejected a delegation from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Silla|qq=}} that had unilaterally changed its national name.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Tenpyō 7, 2/27</ref>
Agatamori died in {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|qq=}} (737) at age 70, likely due to the smallpox epidemic that claimed many lives, including the Fujiwara brothers.<ref name="naoki" />
== Family ==
* Father: {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Shima|qq=}}<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Tenpyō 9, 6/23</ref> * Children: ** Daughter: Wife of {{ill|Fujiwara no Hamanari|ja|藤原浜成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Hamanari|qq=}}<ref>‘Sonpi Bunmyaku’</ref> ** Daughter: Possibly {{ill|Tanbe no Iratsume|ja|丹比郎女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanbe no Iratsume|qq=}}, wife of {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|ja|大伴旅人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Tabito|qq=}} and mother of {{ill|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|ja|大伴家持|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Yakamochi|qq=}}<ref>Kawakami [1971]</ref>

'''Tajihi no Agatamori''' (多治比縣守) was a court noble and government official during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara period|qq=}}. He was a son of {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Shima|qq=}}, who served as a {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣|1=Minister of the Left|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sadaijin|qq=}} (Minister of the Left). Agatamori played key roles in diplomacy, governance, and military affairs, including serving as {{ill|Envoy to Tang China|ja|遣唐使押使|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Envoy to Tang China|qq=}}, {{ill|Governor of Musashi|ja|武蔵守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Musashi|qq=}}, and {{ill|Seii Taishōgun|ja|征夷大将軍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seii Taishōgun|qq=}}.
== Biography ==
Agatamori was born in the 7th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenji|qq=}}'s reign (668), as recorded in sources like the {{ill|Kugyō Bōnin|ja|Kugyō補任|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō Bōnin|qq=}}, which states he passed away in {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|qq=}} (737) at the age of 70.<ref name="naoki">Naoki [2006: 617]</ref> His family, the {{ill|Tajihi clan|ja|多治比氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi clan|qq=}}, descended from {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Senka|qq=}}, making them one of the prominent noble lineages of the time.<ref>Takashima [1969: 10–11]</ref>
=== Mission to Tang China ===
In {{ill|Keiun 2|ja|慶雲2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiun 2|qq=}} (705), Agatamori was promoted from {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=}} to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}. His career advanced rapidly under the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Genmei|qq=}}, and by {{ill|Wadō 8|ja|和銅8年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 8|qq=}} (715), he had reached {{ill|Jūshi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} and was appointed {{ill|Zōkyūkyō|ja|造宮卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōkyūkyō|qq=}}, overseeing the construction of {{ill|Heijō Palace|ja|平城宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heijō Palace|qq=}}.
In {{ill|Reiki 2|ja|霊亀2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 2|qq=}} (716), Agatamori was appointed Envoy to Tang China (遣唐押使). He departed for China in {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 3|qq=}} (717), leading a large delegation of 557 members, including notable figures such as {{ill|Abe no Nakamaro|ja|阿倍仲麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Nakamaro|qq=}}, {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi no Makibi|qq=}}, and {{ill|Gembō|ja|玄昉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gembō|qq=}}. The delegation reached {{ill|Chang'an|ja|長安|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chang'an|qq=}} in October and returned safely in {{ill|Yōrō 2|ja|養老2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 2|qq=}} (718). Agatamori received further promotions upon his return.<ref>Ueda [2006: 80–83]</ref>
=== Governor and Regional Commander ===
Agatamori was later appointed {{ill|Governor of Musashi|ja|武蔵守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Musashi|qq=}} and subsequently became one of the first {{ill|Inspectors (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inspectors (Ancient Japan)|qq=}}, overseeing the provinces of {{ill|Sagami|ja|相模|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagami|qq=}}, {{ill|Kōzuke|ja|上野|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōzuke|qq=}}, and {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke|qq=}}. He also served as {{ill|Governor of Harima|ja|播磨守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Harima|qq=}}.
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 4|qq=}} (720), he was appointed {{ill|Seii Taishōgun|ja|征夷大将軍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seii Taishōgun|qq=}} to suppress a rebellion by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi|qq=}}, following the assassination of {{ill|Kamu Yamakano|ja|上毛野広人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamu Yamakano|qq=}}, the Inspector of Mutsu Province.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45–46]</ref> His campaign achieved partial success, and he was promoted to {{ill|Jūshi-i|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} upon his return.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 44]</ref>
=== Role in Court Politics ===
In {{ill|Shinki 6|ja|神亀6年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinki 6|qq=}} (729), Agatamori was appointed as a provisional {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議|1=Sangi (Japan)|lt=Sangi|2=ja|3=参議|4=ru|5=Санги|6=fr|7=Sangi_(Japon)|8=ko|9=참의_(일본사)|10=es|11=Sangi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangi|qq=}} during the {{ill|Conspiracy of Prince Nagaya|ja|長屋王の変|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Conspiracy of Prince Nagaya|qq=}} and became a court noble shortly afterward, holding key positions under the {{ill|Fujiwara Four Brothers|ja|藤原四兄弟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Four Brothers|qq=}} regime. He reached the Rank of {{ill|Shōsan-i|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōsan-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} and served as {{ill|Chūnagon|ja|中納言|1=Chūnagon|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūnagon|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūnagon|qq=}}.
In {{ill|Tenpyō 7|ja|天平7年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 7|qq=}} (735), he rejected a delegation from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Silla|qq=}} that had unilaterally changed its national name.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Tenpyō 7, 2/27</ref>
Agatamori died in {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|qq=}} (737) at age 70, likely due to the smallpox epidemic that claimed many lives, including the Fujiwara brothers.<ref name="naoki" />
== Family ==
* Father: {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Shima|qq=}}<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Tenpyō 9, 6/23</ref> * Children: ** Daughter: Wife of {{ill|Fujiwara no Hamanari|ja|藤原浜成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Hamanari|qq=}}<ref>‘Sonpi Bunmyaku’</ref> ** Daughter: Possibly {{ill|Tanbe no Iratsume|ja|丹比郎女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanbe no Iratsume|qq=}}, wife of {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|ja|大伴旅人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Tabito|qq=}} and mother of {{ill|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|ja|大伴家持|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Yakamochi|qq=}}<ref>Kawakami [1971]</ref>

Agatamori played key roles in diplomacy, governance, and military affairs, including serving as {{ill|Envoy to Tang China|ja|遣唐使押使|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Envoy to Tang China|qq=}}, {{ill|Governor of Musashi|ja|武蔵守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Musashi|qq=}}, and [[:en:Shogun|Seii Taishōgun]].

Agatamori was born in the 7th year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]]'s reign (668), as recorded in sources like the {{ill|Kugyō Bōnin|ja|Kugyō補任|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō Bōnin|qq=}}, which states he passed away in {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|qq=}} (737) at the age of 70.<ref name="naoki">Naoki [2006: 617]</ref> His family, the [[:en:Tajihi clan|Tajihi clan]], descended from [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]], making them one of the prominent noble lineages of the time.<ref>Takashima [1969: 10–11]</ref>
=== Mission to Tang China ===
In {{ill|Keiun 2|ja|慶雲2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiun 2|qq=}} (705), Agatamori was promoted from {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=}} to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}. His career advanced rapidly under the reign of [[:en:Empress Genmei|Empress Genmei]], and by {{ill|Wadō 8|ja|和銅8年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 8|qq=}} (715), he had reached {{ill|Jūshi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} and was appointed {{ill|Zōkyūkyō|ja|造宮卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōkyūkyō|qq=}}, overseeing the construction of [[:en:Heijō Palace|Heijō Palace]].
In {{ill|Reiki 2|ja|霊亀2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 2|qq=}} (716), Agatamori was appointed Envoy to Tang China (遣唐押使). He departed for China in {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 3|qq=}} (717), leading a large delegation of 557 members, including notable figures such as [[:en:Abe no Nakamaro|Abe no Nakamaro]], [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]], and [[:en:Genbō|Gembō]]. The delegation reached [[:en:Chang'an|Chang'an]] in October and returned safely in {{ill|Yōrō 2|ja|養老2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 2|qq=}} (718). Agatamori received further promotions upon his return.<ref>Ueda [2006: 80–83]</ref>
=== Governor and Regional Commander ===
Agatamori was later appointed {{ill|Governor of Musashi|ja|武蔵守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Musashi|qq=}} and subsequently became one of the first {{ill|Inspectors (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inspectors (Ancient Japan)|qq=}}, overseeing the provinces of [[:en:Sagami|Sagami]], [[:en:Ueno|Kōzuke]], and {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke|qq=}}. He also served as {{ill|Governor of Harima|ja|播磨守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Harima|qq=}}.
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 4|qq=}} (720), he was appointed [[:en:Shogun|Seii Taishōgun]] to suppress a rebellion by the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]], following the assassination of {{ill|Kamu Yamakano|ja|上毛野広人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamu Yamakano|qq=}}, the Inspector of Mutsu Province.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45–46]</ref> His campaign achieved partial success, and he was promoted to {{ill|Jūshi-i|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} upon his return.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 44]</ref>
=== Role in Court Politics ===
In {{ill|Shinki 6|ja|神亀6年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinki 6|qq=}} (729), Agatamori was appointed as a provisional [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] during the {{ill|Conspiracy of Prince Nagaya|ja|長屋王の変|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Conspiracy of Prince Nagaya|qq=}} and became a court noble shortly afterward, holding key positions under the {{ill|Fujiwara Four Brothers|ja|藤原四兄弟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Four Brothers|qq=}} regime. He reached the Rank of {{ill|Shōsan-i|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōsan-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} and served as [[:en:Chūnagon|Chūnagon]].
In {{ill|Tenpyō 7|ja|天平7年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 7|qq=}} (735), he rejected a delegation from [[:en:Silla|Silla]] that had unilaterally changed its national name.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Tenpyō 7, 2/27</ref>
Agatamori died in {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|qq=}} (737) at age 70, likely due to the smallpox epidemic that claimed many lives, including the Fujiwara brothers.<ref name="naoki" />
== Family ==
* Father: {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Shima|qq=}}<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Tenpyō 9, 6/23</ref> * Children: ** Daughter: Wife of [[:en:Fujiwara no Hamanari|Fujiwara no Hamanari]]<ref>‘Sonpi Bunmyaku’</ref> ** Daughter: Possibly {{ill|Tanbe no Iratsume|ja|丹比郎女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanbe no Iratsume|qq=}}, wife of [[:en:Ōtomo no Tabito|Ōtomo no Tabito]] and mother of [[:en:Ōtomo no Yakamochi|Ōtomo no Yakamochi]]<ref>Kawakami [1971]</ref>

[[sn:Tajihi no Agatamori]] [[ja:多治比縣守]] [[en:Tajihi no Agatamori]]
<!--Tajihi no Agatamori--><!--Tajihi no Agatamori--><!--Tajihi no Agatamori--><!--Tajihi no Agatamori--> [[Category:668 births]] [[Category:737 deaths]] [[Category:多治比氏]]

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:奈良時代の貴族
revision.text:
'''Tajihi no Agatamori''' (多治比縣守) was a court noble and government official during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. He was a son of {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Shima|qq=}}, who served as a [[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]] (Minister of the Left). Agatamori played key roles in diplomacy, governance, and military affairs, including serving as {{ill|Envoy to Tang China|ja|遣唐使押使|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Envoy to Tang China|qq=}}, {{ill|Governor of Musashi|ja|武蔵守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Musashi|qq=}}, and [[:en:Shogun|Seii Taishōgun]].
== Biography ==
Agatamori was born in the 7th year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]]'s reign (668), as recorded in sources like the {{ill|Kugyō Bōnin|ja|Kugyō補任|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō Bōnin|qq=}}, which states he passed away in {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|qq=}} (737) at the age of 70.<ref name="naoki">Naoki [2006: 617]</ref> His family, the [[:en:Tajihi clan|Tajihi clan]], descended from [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]], making them one of the prominent noble lineages of the time.<ref>Takashima [1969: 10–11]</ref>
=== Mission to Tang China ===
In {{ill|Keiun 2|ja|慶雲2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiun 2|qq=}} (705), Agatamori was promoted from {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=}} to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}. His career advanced rapidly under the reign of [[:en:Empress Genmei|Empress Genmei]], and by {{ill|Wadō 8|ja|和銅8年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 8|qq=}} (715), he had reached {{ill|Jūshi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} and was appointed {{ill|Zōkyūkyō|ja|造宮卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōkyūkyō|qq=}}, overseeing the construction of [[:en:Heijō Palace|Heijō Palace]].
In {{ill|Reiki 2|ja|霊亀2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 2|qq=}} (716), Agatamori was appointed Envoy to Tang China (遣唐押使). He departed for China in {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 3|qq=}} (717), leading a large delegation of 557 members, including notable figures such as [[:en:Abe no Nakamaro|Abe no Nakamaro]], [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]], and [[:en:Genbō|Gembō]]. The delegation reached [[:en:Chang'an|Chang'an]] in October and returned safely in {{ill|Yōrō 2|ja|養老2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 2|qq=}} (718). Agatamori received further promotions upon his return.<ref>Ueda [2006: 80–83]</ref>
=== Governor and Regional Commander ===
Agatamori was later appointed {{ill|Governor of Musashi|ja|武蔵守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Musashi|qq=}} and subsequently became one of the first {{ill|Inspectors (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inspectors (Ancient Japan)|qq=}}, overseeing the provinces of [[:en:Sagami|Sagami]], [[:en:Ueno|Kōzuke]], and {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke|qq=}}. He also served as {{ill|Governor of Harima|ja|播磨守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Harima|qq=}}.
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 4|qq=}} (720), he was appointed [[:en:Shogun|Seii Taishōgun]] to suppress a rebellion by the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]], following the assassination of {{ill|Kamu Yamakano|ja|上毛野広人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamu Yamakano|qq=}}, the Inspector of Mutsu Province.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45–46]</ref> His campaign achieved partial success, and he was promoted to {{ill|Jūshi-i|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} upon his return.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 44]</ref>
=== Role in Court Politics ===
In {{ill|Shinki 6|ja|神亀6年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinki 6|qq=}} (729), Agatamori was appointed as a provisional [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] during the {{ill|Conspiracy of Prince Nagaya|ja|長屋王の変|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Conspiracy of Prince Nagaya|qq=}} and became a court noble shortly afterward, holding key positions under the {{ill|Fujiwara Four Brothers|ja|藤原四兄弟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Four Brothers|qq=}} regime. He reached the Rank of {{ill|Shōsan-i|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōsan-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} and served as [[:en:Chūnagon|Chūnagon]].
In {{ill|Tenpyō 7|ja|天平7年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 7|qq=}} (735), he rejected a delegation from [[:en:Silla|Silla]] that had unilaterally changed its national name.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Tenpyō 7, 2/27</ref>
Agatamori died in {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|qq=}} (737) at age 70, likely due to the smallpox epidemic that claimed many lives, including the Fujiwara brothers.<ref name="naoki" />
== Family ==
* Father: {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Shima|qq=}}<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Tenpyō 9, 6/23</ref> * Children: ** Daughter: Wife of [[:en:Fujiwara no Hamanari|Fujiwara no Hamanari]]<ref>‘Sonpi Bunmyaku’</ref> ** Daughter: Possibly {{ill|Tanbe no Iratsume|ja|丹比郎女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanbe no Iratsume|qq=}}, wife of [[:en:Ōtomo no Tabito|Ōtomo no Tabito]] and mother of [[:en:Ōtomo no Yakamochi|Ōtomo no Yakamochi]]<ref>Kawakami [1971]</ref>
== References ==
{{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography ==
* Ueda, Yū. *{{ill|遣唐使全航海|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/遣唐使全航海|qq=}}*. Sōshisha, 2006. ISBN 978-4794215444. * Suzuki, Takuya. *{{ill|Emishi to Tōhoku Sensō|ja|蝦夷と東北戦争|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi to Tōhoku Sensō|qq=}}*. Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2008. ISBN 978-4642063135. * Naoki, Kōjirō. *Nihon Kodai Chūsei Jinmei Jiten*. Yoshikawa Kōbu

Agatamori died in {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|qq=}} (737) at age 70, likely due to the smallpox epidemic that claimed many lives, including the Fujiwara brothers.<ref name="naoki" />
== Family ==
* Father: {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Shima|qq=}}<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Tenpyō 9, 6/23</ref> * Children: ** Daughter: Wife of [[:en:Fujiwara no Hamanari|Fujiwara no Hamanari]]<ref>‘Sonpi Bunmyaku’</ref> ** Daughter: Possibly {{ill|Tanbe no Iratsume|ja|丹比郎女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanbe no Iratsume|qq=}}, wife of [[:en:Ōtomo no Tabito|Ōtomo no Tabito]] and mother of [[:en:Ōtomo no Yakamochi|Ōtomo no Yakamochi]]<ref>Kawakami [1971]</ref>

[[sn:Tajihi no Agatamori]] [[ja:多治比縣守]]
<!--Tajihi no Agatamori--><!--Tajihi no Agatamori--><!--Tajihi no Agatamori--><!--Tajihi no Agatamori--> [[Category:668 births]] [[Category:737 deaths]] [[Category:多治比氏]] [[Category:奈良時代の貴族]] [[Category:Japanese ambassadors to the Tang dynasty]]

'''Tajihi no Agatamori''' (多治比縣守) was a court noble and government official during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. He was a son of {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Shima|qq=}}, who served as a [[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]] (Minister of the Left). Agatamori played key roles in diplomacy, governance, and military affairs, including serving as {{ill|Envoy to Tang China|ja|遣唐使押使|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Envoy to Tang China|qq=}}, {{ill|Governor of Musashi|ja|武蔵守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Musashi|qq=}}, and [[:en:Shogun|Seii Taishōgun]].
== Biography ==
Agatamori was born in the 7th year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]]'s reign (668), as recorded in sources like the {{ill|Kugyō Bōnin|ja|Kugyō補任|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō Bōnin|qq=}}, which states he passed away in {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|qq=}} (737) at the age of 70.<ref name="naoki">Naoki [2006: 617]</ref> His family, the [[:en:Tajihi clan|Tajihi clan]], descended from [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]], making them one of the prominent noble lineages of the time.<ref>Takashima [1969: 10–11]</ref>
=== Mission to Tang China ===
In {{ill|Keiun 2|ja|慶雲2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiun 2|qq=}} (705), Agatamori was promoted from {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=}} to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}. His career advanced rapidly under the reign of [[:en:Empress Genmei|Empress Genmei]], and by {{ill|Wadō 8|ja|和銅8年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 8|qq=}} (715), he had reached {{ill|Jūshi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} and was appointed {{ill|Zōkyūkyō|ja|造宮卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōkyūkyō|qq=}}, overseeing the construction of [[:en:Heijō Palace|Heijō Palace]].
In {{ill|Reiki 2|ja|霊亀2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 2|qq=}} (716), Agatamori was appointed Envoy to Tang China (遣唐押使). He departed for China in {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 3|qq=}} (717), leading a large delegation of 557 members, including notable figures such as [[:en:Abe no Nakamaro|Abe no Nakamaro]], [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]], and [[:en:Genbō|Gembō]]. The delegation reached [[:en:Chang'an|Chang'an]] in October and returned safely in {{ill|Yōrō 2|ja|養老2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 2|qq=}} (718). Agatamori received further promotions upon his return.<ref>Ueda [2006: 80–83]</ref>
=== Governor and Regional Commander ===
Agatamori was later appointed {{ill|Governor of Musashi|ja|武蔵守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Musashi|qq=}} and subsequently became one of the first {{ill|Inspectors (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inspectors (Ancient Japan)|qq=}}, overseeing the provinces of [[:en:Sagami|Sagami]], [[:en:Ueno|Kōzuke]], and {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke|qq=}}. He also served as {{ill|Governor of Harima|ja|播磨守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Harima|qq=}}.
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 4|qq=}} (720), he was appointed [[:en:Shogun|Seii Taishōgun]] to suppress a rebellion by the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]], following the assassination of {{ill|Kamu Yamakano|ja|上毛野広人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamu Yamakano|qq=}}, the Inspector of Mutsu Province.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45–46]</ref> His campaign achieved partial success, and he was promoted to {{ill|Jūshi-i|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} upon his return.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 44]</ref>
=== Role in Court Politics ===
In {{ill|Shinki 6|ja|神亀6年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinki 6|qq=}} (729), Agatamori was appointed as a provisional [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] during the {{ill|Conspiracy of Prince Nagaya|ja|長屋王の変|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Conspiracy of Prince Nagaya|qq=}} and became a court noble shortly afterward, holding key positions under the {{ill|Fujiwara Four Brothers|ja|藤原四兄弟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Four Brothers|qq=}} regime. He reached the Rank of {{ill|Shōsan-i|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōsan-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} and served as [[:en:Chūnagon|Chūnagon]].
In {{ill|Tenpyō 7|ja|天平7年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 7|qq=}} (735), he rejected a delegation from [[:en:Silla|Silla]] that had unilaterally changed its national name.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Tenpyō 7, 2/27</ref>
Agatamori died in {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|qq=}} (737) at age 70, likely due to the smallpox epidemic that claimed many lives, including the Fujiwara brothers.<ref name="naoki" />
== Family ==
* Father: {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Shima|qq=}}<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Tenpyō 9, 6/23</ref> * Children: ** Daughter: Wife of [[:en:Fujiwara no Hamanari|Fujiwara no Hamanari]]<ref>‘Sonpi Bunmyaku’</ref> ** Daughter: Possibly {{ill|Tanbe no Iratsume|ja|丹比郎女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanbe no Iratsume|qq=}}, wife of [[:en:Ōtomo no Tabito|Ōtomo no Tabito]] and mother of [[:en:Ōtomo no Yakamochi|Ōtomo no Yakamochi]]<ref>Kawakami [1971]</ref>
== References ==
{{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography ==
* Ueda, Yū. *{{ill|遣唐使全航海|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/遣唐使全航海|qq=}}*. Sōshisha, 2006. ISBN 978-4794215444. * Suzuki, Takuya. *{{ill|Emishi to Tōhoku Sensō|ja|蝦夷と東北戦争|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi to Tōhoku Sensō|qq=}}*. Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2008. He departed for China in {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 3|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (717), leading a large delegation of 557 members, including notable figures such as [[:en:Abe no Nakamaro|Abe no Nakamaro]], [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]], and [[:en:Genbō|Gembō]]. The delegation reached [[:en:Chang'an|Chang'an]] in October and returned safely in {{ill|Yōrō 2|ja|養老2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 2|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (718). Agatamori received further promotions upon his return.<ref>Ueda [2006: 80–83]</ref>
=== Governor and Regional Commander ===
Agatamori was later appointed {{ill|Governor of Musashi|ja|武蔵守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Musashi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武蔵国司}} and subsequently became one of the first {{ill|Inspectors (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inspectors (Ancient Japan)|qq=}}, overseeing the provinces of [[:en:Sagami|Sagami]], [[:en:Ueno|Kōzuke]], and {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke|qq=}}. He also served as {{ill|Governor of Harima|ja|播磨守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Harima|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 播磨国}}.
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 4|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (720), he was appointed [[:en:Shogun|Seii Taishōgun]] to suppress a rebellion by the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]], following the assassination of {{ill|Kamu Yamakano|ja|上毛野広人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamu Yamakano|qq=}}, the Inspector of Mutsu Province.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45–46]</ref> His campaign achieved partial success, and he was promoted to {{ill|Jūshi-i|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} upon his return.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 44]</ref>
=== Role in Court Politics ===
In {{ill|Shinki 6|ja|神亀6年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinki 6|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (729), Agatamori was appointed as a provisional [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] during the {{ill|Conspiracy of Prince Nagaya|ja|長屋王の変|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Conspiracy of Prince Nagaya|qq=}} and became a court noble shortly afterward, holding key positions under the {{ill|Fujiwara Four Brothers|ja|藤原四兄弟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Four Brothers|qq=}} regime. He reached the Rank of {{ill|Shōsan-i|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōsan-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} and served as [[:en:Chūnagon|Chūnagon]].
In {{ill|Tenpyō 7|ja|天平7年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 7|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (735), he rejected a delegation from [[:en:Silla|Silla]] that had unilaterally changed its national name.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Tenpyō 7, 2/27</ref>
Agatamori died in {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (737) at age 70, likely due to the smallpox epidemic that claimed many lives, including the Fujiwara brothers.<ref name="naoki" />
== Family ==
* Father: {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Shima|qq=}}<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Tenpyō 9, 6/23</ref> * Children: ** Daughter: Wife of [[:en:Fujiwara no Hamanari|Fujiwara no Hamanari]]<ref>‘Sonpi Bunmyaku’</ref> ** Daughter: Possibly {{ill|Tanbe no Iratsume|ja|丹比郎女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanbe no Iratsume|qq=}}, wife of [[:en:Ōtomo no Tabito|Ōtomo no Tabito]] and mother of [[:en:Ōtomo no Yakamochi|Ōtomo no Yakamochi]]<ref>Kawakami [1971]</ref>

== Bibliography ==
* Ueda, Yū. *{{ill|遣唐使全航海|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/遣唐使全航海|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*. Sōshisha, 2006. ISBN 978-4794215444. * Suzuki, Takuya. *{{ill|Emishi to Tōhoku Sensō|ja|蝦夷と東北戦争|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi to Tōhoku Sensō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*. Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2008. ISBN 978-4642063135. * Naoki, Kōjirō. *Nihon Kodai Chūsei Jinmei Jiten*. Yoshikawa Kōbu
