<!--Tajihi no Agatamori--><!--Tajihi no Agatamori--><!--Tajihi no Agatamori--><!--Tajihi no Agatamori--> [[Category:668 births]] [[Category:737 deaths]] [[Category:Tajihi Clan]] [[Category:Aristocrats of the Nara period]] [[Category:Japanese ambassadors to the Tang dynasty]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[fa:تاجیهی نو آگاتاموری]] [[uk:Тадзіхі но Аґатаморі]] [[zh:多治比县守]] {{wikidata link|Q11430840}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fa:تاجیهی نو آگاتاموری]][[ja:多治比縣守]][[uk:Тадзіхі но Аґатаморі]][[zh:多治比县守]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fa:تاجیهی نو آگاتاموری]][[ja:多治比縣守]][[uk:Тадзіхі но Аґатаморі]][[zh:多治比县守]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0668|death_date={{death date|0737|7|25}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Q106191660|WD=Q106191660}}; {{ill|Q11368537|WD=Q11368537}}; {{ill|Q18048350|WD=Q18048350}}|father={{ill|Tajihi no Shima|WD=Q11430835}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11430840]] Wikidata name: Tajihi no Agatamori [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tajihi no Agatamori''' (多治比縣守) was a court noble and government official during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. He was a son of {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Shima|qq=}}, who served as a [[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]] (Minister of the Left). Agatamori played key roles in diplomacy, governance, and military affairs, including serving as {{ill|Envoy to Tang China|ja|遣唐使押使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Envoy to Tang China|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Governor of Musashi|ja|武蔵守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Musashi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武蔵国司|1=musashikokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546337}}, and [[:en:Shogun|Seii Taishōgun]].
== Biography ==
Agatamori was born in the 7th year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]]'s reign (668), as recorded in sources like the {{ill|Kugyō Bōnin|ja|Kugyō補任|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō Bōnin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which states he passed away in {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (737) at the age of 70.<ref name="naoki">Naoki [2006: 617]</ref> His family, the [[:en:Tajihi clan|Tajihi clan]], descended from [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]], making them one of the prominent noble lineages of the time.<ref>Takashima [1969: 10–11]</ref>
=== Mission to Tang China ===
In {{ill|Keiun 2|ja|慶雲2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiun 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (705), Agatamori was promoted from {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=}} to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. His career advanced rapidly under the reign of [[:en:Empress Genmei|Empress Genmei]], and by {{ill|Wadō 8|ja|和銅8年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 8|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (715), he had reached {{ill|Jūshi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} and was appointed {{ill|Zōkyūkyō|ja|造宮卿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōkyūkyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造宮省|1=Zōgūshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11639741}}, overseeing the construction of [[:en:Heijō Palace|Heijō Palace]].
In {{ill|Reiki 2|ja|霊亀2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (716), Agatamori was appointed Envoy to Tang China (遣唐押使). He departed for China in {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (717), leading a large delegation of 557 members, including notable figures such as [[:en:Abe no Nakamaro|Abe no Nakamaro]], [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]], and [[:en:Genbō|Gembō]]. The delegation reached [[:en:Chang'an|Chang'an]] in October and returned safely in {{ill|Yōrō 2|ja|養老2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (718). Agatamori received further promotions upon his return.<ref>Ueda [2006: 80–83]</ref>
=== Governor and Regional Commander ===
Agatamori was later appointed {{ill|Governor of Musashi|ja|武蔵守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Musashi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武蔵国司|1=musashikokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546337}} and subsequently became one of the first {{ill|Inspectors (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inspectors (Ancient Japan)|1=Provincial Judge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q848663}}, overseeing the provinces of [[:en:Sagami|Sagami]], [[:en:Ueno|Kōzuke]], and {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke|1=Shimotsuke Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1208689}}. He also served as {{ill|Governor of Harima|ja|播磨守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Harima|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 播磨国|1=Harima Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1047523}}.
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (720), he was appointed [[:en:Shogun|Seii Taishōgun]] to suppress a rebellion by the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]], following the assassination of {{ill|Kamu Yamakano|ja|上毛野広人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamu Yamakano|1=Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q45828852}}, the Inspector of Mutsu Province.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45–46]</ref> His campaign achieved partial success, and he was promoted to {{ill|Jūshi-i|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} upon his return.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 44]</ref>
=== Role in Court Politics ===
In {{ill|Shinki 6|ja|神亀6年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinki 6|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (729), Agatamori was appointed as a provisional [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] during the {{ill|Conspiracy of Prince Nagaya|ja|長屋王の変|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Conspiracy of Prince Nagaya|1=Prince Nagaya incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109357375}} and became a court noble shortly afterward, holding key positions under the {{ill|Fujiwara Four Brothers|ja|藤原四兄弟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Four Brothers|qq=}} regime. He reached the Rank of {{ill|Shōsan-i|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōsan-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} and served as [[:en:Chūnagon|Chūnagon]].
In {{ill|Tenpyō 7|ja|天平7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 7|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (735), he rejected a delegation from [[:en:Silla|Silla]] that had unilaterally changed its national name.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Tenpyō 7, 2/27</ref>
Agatamori died in {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (737) at age 70, likely due to the smallpox epidemic that claimed many lives, including the Fujiwara brothers.<ref name="naoki" />
== Family ==
* Father: {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Shima|qq=}}<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Tenpyō 9, 6/23</ref> * Children: ** Daughter: Wife of [[:en:Fujiwara no Hamanari|Fujiwara no Hamanari]]<ref>‘Sonpi Bunmyaku’</ref> ** Daughter: Possibly {{ill|Tanbe no Iratsume|ja|丹比郎女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanbe no Iratsume|1=Tambi no Iratsume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368537}}, wife of [[:en:Ōtomo no Tabito|Ōtomo no Tabito]] and mother of [[:en:Ōtomo no Yakamochi|Ōtomo no Yakamochi]]<ref>Kawakami [1971]</ref>

ISBN 978-4794215444. * Suzuki, Takuya. *{{ill|Emishi to Tōhoku Sensō|ja|蝦夷と東北戦争|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi to Tōhoku Sensō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*. Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2008. ISBN 978-4642063135. * Naoki, Kōjirō. *Nihon Kodai Chūsei Jinmei Jiten*. Yoshikawa Kōbu

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fa:تاجیهی نو آگاتاموری]][[ja:多治比縣守]][[uk:Тадзіхі но Аґатаморі]][[zh:多治比县守]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fa:تاجیهی نو آگاتاموری]][[ja:多治比縣守]][[uk:Тадзіхі но Аґатаморі]][[zh:多治比县守]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fa:تاجیهی نو آگاتاموری]][[ja:多治比縣守]][[uk:Тадзіхі но Аґатаморі]][[zh:多治比县守]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0668|death_date={{death date|0737|7|25}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Q106191660|WD=Q106191660}}; {{ill|Q11368537|WD=Q11368537}}; {{ill|Q18048350|WD=Q18048350}}|father={{ill|Tajihi no Shima|WD=Q11430835}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11430840]] Wikidata name: Tajihi no Agatamori [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tajihi no Agatamori''' (多治比縣守) was a court noble and government official during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. He was a son of {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Shima|qq=}}, who served as a [[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]] (Minister of the Left). Agatamori played key roles in diplomacy, governance, and military affairs, including serving as {{ill|Envoy to Tang China|ja|遣唐使押使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Envoy to Tang China|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Governor of Musashi|ja|武蔵守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Musashi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武蔵国司|1=musashikokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546337}}, and [[:en:Shogun|Seii Taishōgun]].
== Biography ==
Agatamori was born in the 7th year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]]'s reign (668), as recorded in sources like the {{ill|Kugyō Bōnin|ja|Kugyō補任|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō Bōnin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which states he passed away in {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (737) at the age of 70.<ref name="naoki">Naoki [2006: 617]</ref> His family, the [[:en:Tajihi clan|Tajihi clan]], descended from [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]], making them one of the prominent noble lineages of the time.<ref>Takashima [1969: 10–11]</ref>
=== Mission to Tang China ===
In {{ill|Keiun 2|ja|慶雲2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiun 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (705), Agatamori was promoted from {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=}} to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. His career advanced rapidly under the reign of [[:en:Empress Genmei|Empress Genmei]], and by {{ill|Wadō 8|ja|和銅8年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 8|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (715), he had reached {{ill|Jūshi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} and was appointed {{ill|Zōkyūkyō|ja|造宮卿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōkyūkyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造宮省|1=Zōgūshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11639741}}, overseeing the construction of [[:en:Heijō Palace|Heijō Palace]].
In {{ill|Reiki 2|ja|霊亀2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (716), Agatamori was appointed Envoy to Tang China (遣唐押使). He departed for China in {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (717), leading a large delegation of 557 members, including notable figures such as [[:en:Abe no Nakamaro|Abe no Nakamaro]], [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]], and [[:en:Genbō|Gembō]]. The delegation reached [[:en:Chang'an|Chang'an]] in October and returned safely in {{ill|Yōrō 2|ja|養老2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (718). Agatamori received further promotions upon his return.<ref>Ueda [2006: 80–83]</ref>
=== Governor and Regional Commander ===
Agatamori was later appointed {{ill|Governor of Musashi|ja|武蔵守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Musashi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武蔵国司|1=musashikokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546337}} and subsequently became one of the first {{ill|Inspectors (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inspectors (Ancient Japan)|1=Provincial Judge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q848663}}, overseeing the provinces of [[:en:Sagami|Sagami]], [[:en:Ueno|Kōzuke]], and {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke|1=Shimotsuke Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1208689}}. He also served as {{ill|Governor of Harima|ja|播磨守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Harima|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 播磨国|1=Harima Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1047523}}.
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (720), he was appointed [[:en:Shogun|Seii Taishōgun]] to suppress a rebellion by the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]], following the assassination of {{ill|Kamu Yamakano|ja|上毛野広人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamu Yamakano|1=Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q45828852}}, the Inspector of Mutsu Province.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45–46]</ref> His campaign achieved partial success, and he was promoted to {{ill|Jūshi-i|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} upon his return.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 44]</ref>
=== Role in Court Politics ===
In {{ill|Shinki 6|ja|神亀6年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinki 6|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (729), Agatamori was appointed as a provisional [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] during the {{ill|Conspiracy of Prince Nagaya|ja|長屋王の変|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Conspiracy of Prince Nagaya|1=Prince Nagaya incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109357375}} and became a court noble shortly afterward, holding key positions under the {{ill|Fujiwara Four Brothers|ja|藤原四兄弟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Four Brothers|qq=}} regime. He reached the Rank of {{ill|Shōsan-i|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōsan-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} and served as [[:en:Chūnagon|Chūnagon]].
In {{ill|Tenpyō 7|ja|天平7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 7|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (735), he rejected a delegation from [[:en:Silla|Silla]] that had unilaterally changed its national name.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Tenpyō 7, 2/27</ref>
Agatamori died in {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (737) at age 70, likely due to the smallpox epidemic that claimed many lives, including the Fujiwara brothers.<ref name="naoki" />
== Family ==
* Father: {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Shima|qq=}}<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Tenpyō 9, 6/23</ref> * Children: ** Daughter: Wife of [[:en:Fujiwara no Hamanari|Fujiwara no Hamanari]]<ref>‘Sonpi Bunmyaku’</ref> ** Daughter: Possibly {{ill|Tanbe no Iratsume|ja|丹比郎女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanbe no Iratsume|1=Tambi no Iratsume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368537}}, wife of [[:en:Ōtomo no Tabito|Ōtomo no Tabito]] and mother of [[:en:Ōtomo no Yakamochi|Ōtomo no Yakamochi]]<ref>Kawakami [1971]</ref>
== References ==
{{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography ==
* Ueda, Yū. *{{ill|遣唐使全航海|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/遣唐使全航海|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*. Sōshisha, 2006. ISBN 978-4794215444. * Suzuki, Takuya. *{{ill|Emishi to Tōhoku Sensō|ja|蝦夷と東北戦争|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi to Tōhoku Sensō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*. Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2008. ISBN 978-4642063135. * Naoki, Kōjirō. *Nihon Kodai Chūsei Jinmei Jiten*. Yoshikawa Kōbu

He was a son of {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Shima|qq=}}, who served as a [[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]] (Minister of the Left). Agatamori received further promotions upon his return.<ref>Ueda [2006: 80–83]</ref>
=== Governor and Regional Commander ===
Agatamori was later appointed {{ill|Governor of Musashi|ja|武蔵守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Musashi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武蔵国司|1=musashikokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546337}} and subsequently became one of the first {{ill|Inspectors (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inspectors (Ancient Japan)|1=Provincial Judge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q848663}}, overseeing the provinces of [[:en:Sagami|Sagami]], [[:en:Ueno|Kōzuke]], and {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke|1=Shimotsuke Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1208689}}. He also served as {{ill|Governor of Harima|ja|播磨守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Harima|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 播磨国|1=Harima Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1047523}}.

In {{ill|Tenpyō 7|ja|天平7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 7|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (735), he rejected a delegation from [[:en:Silla|Silla]] that had unilaterally changed its national name.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Tenpyō 7, 2/27</ref>
Agatamori died in {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (737) at age 70, likely due to the smallpox epidemic that claimed many lives, including the Fujiwara brothers.<ref name="naoki" />
== Family ==
* Father: {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Shima|qq=}}<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Tenpyō 9, 6/23</ref> * Children: ** Daughter: Wife of [[:en:Fujiwara no Hamanari|Fujiwara no Hamanari]]<ref>‘Sonpi Bunmyaku’</ref> ** Daughter: Possibly {{ill|Tanbe no Iratsume|ja|丹比郎女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanbe no Iratsume|1=Tambi no Iratsume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368537}}, wife of [[:en:Ōtomo no Tabito|Ōtomo no Tabito]] and mother of [[:en:Ōtomo no Yakamochi|Ōtomo no Yakamochi]]<ref>Kawakami [1971]</ref>

=== Mission to Tang China ===
In {{ill|Keiun 2|ja|慶雲2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiun 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (705), Agatamori was promoted from {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=}} to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. His career advanced rapidly under the reign of [[:en:Empress Genmei|Empress Genmei]], and by {{ill|Wadō 8|ja|和銅8年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 8|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (715), he had reached {{ill|Jūshi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} and was appointed {{ill|Zōkyūkyō|ja|造宮卿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōkyūkyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造宮省|1=Zōgūshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11639741}}, overseeing the construction of [[:en:Heijō Palace|Heijō Palace]].
In {{ill|Reiki 2|ja|霊亀2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (716), Agatamori was appointed Envoy to Tang China (遣唐押使). He departed for China in {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (717), leading a large delegation of 557 members, including notable figures such as [[:en:Abe no Nakamaro|Abe no Nakamaro]], [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]], and [[:en:Genbō|Gembō]]. The delegation reached [[:en:Chang'an|Chang'an]] in October and returned safely in {{ill|Yōrō 2|ja|養老2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (718). Agatamori received further promotions upon his return.<ref>Ueda [2006: 80–83]</ref>
=== Governor and Regional Commander ===
Agatamori was later appointed {{ill|Governor of Musashi|ja|武蔵守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Musashi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武蔵国司|1=musashikokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546337}} and subsequently became one of the first {{ill|Inspectors (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inspectors (Ancient Japan)|1=Provincial Judge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q848663}}, overseeing the provinces of [[:en:Sagami|Sagami]], [[:en:Ueno|Kōzuke]], and {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke|1=Shimotsuke Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1208689}}. He also served as {{ill|Governor of Harima|ja|播磨守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Harima|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 播磨国|1=Harima Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1047523}}.
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (720), he was appointed [[:en:Shogun|Seii Taishōgun]] to suppress a rebellion by the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]], following the assassination of {{ill|Kamu Yamakano|ja|上毛野広人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamu Yamakano|1=Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q45828852}}, the Inspector of Mutsu Province.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45–46]</ref> His campaign achieved partial success, and he was promoted to {{ill|Jūshi-i|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} upon his return.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 44]</ref>
=== Role in Court Politics ===
In {{ill|Shinki 6|ja|神亀6年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinki 6|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (729), Agatamori was appointed as a provisional [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] during the {{ill|Conspiracy of Prince Nagaya|ja|長屋王の変|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Conspiracy of Prince Nagaya|1=Prince Nagaya incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109357375}} and became a court noble shortly afterward, holding key positions under the {{ill|Fujiwara Four Brothers|ja|藤原四兄弟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Four Brothers|qq=}} regime. He reached the Rank of {{ill|Shōsan-i|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōsan-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} and served as [[:en:Chūnagon|Chūnagon]].
In {{ill|Tenpyō 7|ja|天平7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 7|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (735), he rejected a delegation from [[:en:Silla|Silla]] that had unilaterally changed its national name.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Tenpyō 7, 2/27</ref>
Agatamori died in {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (737) at age 70, likely due to the smallpox epidemic that claimed many lives, including the Fujiwara brothers.<ref name="naoki" />
== Family ==
* Father: {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Shima|qq=}}<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Tenpyō 9, 6/23</ref> * Children: ** Daughter: Wife of [[:en:Fujiwara no Hamanari|Fujiwara no Hamanari]]<ref>‘Sonpi Bunmyaku’</ref> ** Daughter: Possibly {{ill|Tanbe no Iratsume|ja|丹比郎女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanbe no Iratsume|1=Tambi no Iratsume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368537}}, wife of [[:en:Ōtomo no Tabito|Ōtomo no Tabito]] and mother of [[:en:Ōtomo no Yakamochi|Ōtomo no Yakamochi]]<ref>Kawakami [1971]</ref>

ISBN 978-4794215444. * Suzuki, Takuya. *{{ill|Emishi to Tōhoku Sensō|ja|蝦夷と東北戦争|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi to Tōhoku Sensō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*. Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2008. He was a son of {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Shima|qq=}}, who served as a [[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]] (Minister of the Left). Agatamori played key roles in diplomacy, governance, and military affairs, including serving as {{ill|Envoy to Tang China|ja|遣唐使押使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Envoy to Tang China|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Governor of Musashi|ja|武蔵守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Musashi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武蔵国司|1=musashikokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546337}}, and [[:en:Shogun|Seii Taishōgun]].
== Biography ==
Agatamori was born in the 7th year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]]'s reign (668), as recorded in sources like the {{ill|Kugyō Bōnin|ja|Kugyō補任|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō Bōnin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which states he passed away in {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (737) at the age of 70.<ref name="naoki">Naoki [2006: 617]</ref> His family, the [[:en:Tajihi clan|Tajihi clan]], descended from [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]], making them one of the prominent noble lineages of the time.<ref>Takashima [1969: 10–11]</ref>
=== Mission to Tang China ===
In {{ill|Keiun 2|ja|慶雲2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiun 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (705), Agatamori was promoted from {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=}} to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. His career advanced rapidly under the reign of [[:en:Empress Genmei|Empress Genmei]], and by {{ill|Wadō 8|ja|和銅8年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 8|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (715), he had reached {{ill|Jūshi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} and was appointed {{ill|Zōkyūkyō|ja|造宮卿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōkyūkyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造宮省|1=Zōgūshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11639741}}, overseeing the construction of [[:en:Heijō Palace|Heijō Palace]].
In {{ill|Reiki 2|ja|霊亀2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (716), Agatamori was appointed Envoy to Tang China (遣唐押使). He departed for China in {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (717), leading a large delegation of 557 members, including notable figures such as [[:en:Abe no Nakamaro|Abe no Nakamaro]], [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]], and [[:en:Genbō|Gembō]]. The delegation reached [[:en:Chang'an|Chang'an]] in October and returned safely in {{ill|Yōrō 2|ja|養老2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (718). Agatamori received further promotions upon his return.<ref>Ueda [2006: 80–83]</ref>
=== Governor and Regional Commander ===
Agatamori was later appointed {{ill|Governor of Musashi|ja|武蔵守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Musashi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武蔵国司|1=musashikokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546337}} and subsequently became one of the first {{ill|Inspectors (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inspectors (Ancient Japan)|1=Provincial Judge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q848663}}, overseeing the provinces of [[:en:Sagami|Sagami]], [[:en:Ueno|Kōzuke]], and {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke|1=Shimotsuke Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1208689}}. He also served as {{ill|Governor of Harima|ja|播磨守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Harima|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 播磨国|1=Harima Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1047523}}.
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (720), he was appointed [[:en:Shogun|Seii Taishōgun]] to suppress a rebellion by the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]], following the assassination of {{ill|Kamu Yamakano|ja|上毛野広人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamu Yamakano|1=Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q45828852}}, the Inspector of Mutsu Province.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45–46]</ref> His campaign achieved partial success, and he was promoted to {{ill|Jūshi-i|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} upon his return.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 44]</ref>

Agatamori received further promotions upon his return.<ref>Ueda [2006: 80–83]</ref>
=== Governor and Regional Commander ===
Agatamori was later appointed {{ill|Governor of Musashi|ja|武蔵守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Musashi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武蔵国司|1=musashikokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546337}} and subsequently became one of the first {{ill|Inspectors (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inspectors (Ancient Japan)|1=Provincial Judge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q848663}}, overseeing the provinces of {{ill|Sagami|en|Sagami|WD=Q524473|lt=Sagami}}, {{ill|Ueno|en|Ueno|WD=Q576781|lt=Kōzuke}}, and {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke|1=Shimotsuke Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1208689}}. He also served as {{ill|Governor of Harima|ja|播磨守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Harima|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 播磨国|1=Harima Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1047523}}.
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (720), he was appointed {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Seii Taishōgun}} to suppress a rebellion by the {{ill|Emishi|en|Emishi|WD=Q932574|lt=Emishi}}, following the assassination of {{ill|Kamu Yamakano|ja|上毛野広人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamu Yamakano|1=Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q45828852}}, the Inspector of Mutsu Province.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45–46]</ref> His campaign achieved partial success, and he was promoted to {{ill|Jūshi-i|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} upon his return.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 44]</ref>
=== Role in Court Politics ===
In {{ill|Shinki 6|ja|神亀6年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinki 6|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (729), Agatamori was appointed as a provisional {{ill|Sangi (Japan)|en|Sangi (Japan)|WD=Q1133663|lt=Sangi}} during the {{ill|Conspiracy of Prince Nagaya|ja|長屋王の変|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Conspiracy of Prince Nagaya|1=Prince Nagaya incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109357375}} and became a court noble shortly afterward, holding key positions under the {{ill|Fujiwara Four Brothers|ja|藤原四兄弟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Four Brothers|qq=}} regime. He reached the Rank of {{ill|Shōsan-i|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōsan-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} and served as {{ill|Chūnagon|en|Chūnagon|WD=Q1185382|lt=Chūnagon}}.
In {{ill|Tenpyō 7|ja|天平7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 7|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (735), he rejected a delegation from {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla}} that had unilaterally changed its national name.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Tenpyō 7, 2/27</ref>
Agatamori died in {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (737) at age 70, likely due to the smallpox epidemic that claimed many lives, including the Fujiwara brothers.<ref name="naoki" />
== Family ==
* Father: {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Shima|qq=}}<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Tenpyō 9, 6/23</ref> * Children: ** Daughter: Wife of {{ill|Fujiwara no Hamanari|en|Fujiwara no Hamanari|WD=Q3090622|lt=Fujiwara no Hamanari}}<ref>‘Sonpi Bunmyaku’</ref> ** Daughter: Possibly {{ill|Tanbe no Iratsume|ja|丹比郎女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanbe no Iratsume|1=Tambi no Iratsume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368537}}, wife of {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|en|Ōtomo no Tabito|WD=Q386287|lt=Ōtomo no Tabito}} and mother of {{ill|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|en|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|WD=Q386308|lt=Ōtomo no Yakamochi}}<ref>Kawakami [1971]</ref>

ISBN 978-4794215444. * Suzuki, Takuya. *{{ill|Emishi to Tōhoku Sensō|ja|蝦夷と東北戦争|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi to Tōhoku Sensō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*. Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2008. <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fa:تاجیهی نو آگاتاموری]][[ja:多治比縣守]][[uk:Тадзіхі но Аґатаморі]][[zh:多治比县守]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fa:تاجیهی نو آگاتاموری]][[ja:多治比縣守]][[uk:Тадзіхі но Аґатаморі]][[zh:多治比县守]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fa:تاجیهی نو آگاتاموری]][[ja:多治比縣守]][[uk:Тадзіхі но Аґатаморі]][[zh:多治比县守]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0668|death_date={{death date|0737|7|25}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Q106191660|WD=Q106191660}}; {{ill|Q11368537|WD=Q11368537}}; {{ill|Q18048350|WD=Q18048350}}|father={{ill|Tajihi no Shima|WD=Q11430835}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11430840]] Wikidata name: Tajihi no Agatamori [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tajihi no Agatamori''' (多治比縣守) was a court noble and government official during the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}}. He was a son of {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Shima|qq=}}, who served as a {{ill|Minister of the Left|en|Minister of the Left|WD=Q949008|lt=Minister of the Left}} (Minister of the Left). Agatamori played key roles in diplomacy, governance, and military affairs, including serving as {{ill|Envoy to Tang China|ja|遣唐使押使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Envoy to Tang China|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Governor of Musashi|ja|武蔵守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Musashi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武蔵国司|1=musashikokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546337}}, and {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Seii Taishōgun}}.
== Biography ==
Agatamori was born in the 7th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji}}'s reign (668), as recorded in sources like the {{ill|Kugyō Bōnin|ja|Kugyō補任|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō Bōnin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which states he passed away in {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (737) at the age of 70.<ref name="naoki">Naoki [2006: 617]</ref> His family, the {{ill|Tajihi clan|en|Tajihi clan|WD=Q11430839|lt=Tajihi clan}}, descended from {{ill|Emperor Senka|en|Emperor Senka|WD=Q329728|lt=Emperor Senka}}, making them one of the prominent noble lineages of the time.<ref>Takashima [1969: 10–11]</ref>
=== Mission to Tang China ===
In {{ill|Keiun 2|ja|慶雲2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiun 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (705), Agatamori was promoted from {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=}} to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. His career advanced rapidly under the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|en|Empress Genmei|WD=Q233224|lt=Empress Genmei}}, and by {{ill|Wadō 8|ja|和銅8年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 8|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (715), he had reached {{ill|Jūshi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} and was appointed {{ill|Zōkyūkyō|ja|造宮卿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōkyūkyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造宮省|1=Zōgūshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11639741}}, overseeing the construction of {{ill|Heijō Palace|en|Heijō Palace|WD=Q211585|lt=Heijō Palace}}.
In {{ill|Reiki 2|ja|霊亀2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (716), Agatamori was appointed Envoy to Tang China (遣唐押使). He departed for China in {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (717), leading a large delegation of 557 members, including notable figures such as {{ill|Abe no Nakamaro|en|Abe no Nakamaro|WD=Q479788|lt=Abe no Nakamaro}}, {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|en|Kibi no Makibi|WD=Q1063276|lt=Kibi no Makibi}}, and {{ill|Genbō|en|Genbō|WD=Q657737|lt=Gembō}}. The delegation reached {{ill|Chang'an|en|Chang'an|WD=Q6501000|lt=Chang'an}} in October and returned safely in {{ill|Yōrō 2|ja|養老2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (718). Agatamori received further promotions upon his return.<ref>Ueda [2006: 80–83]</ref>
=== Governor and Regional Commander ===
Agatamori was later appointed {{ill|Governor of Musashi|ja|武蔵守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Musashi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武蔵国司|1=musashikokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546337}} and subsequently became one of the first {{ill|Inspectors (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inspectors (Ancient Japan)|1=Provincial Judge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q848663}}, overseeing the provinces of {{ill|Sagami|en|Sagami|WD=Q524473|lt=Sagami}}, {{ill|Ueno|en|Ueno|WD=Q576781|lt=Kōzuke}}, and {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke|1=Shimotsuke Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1208689}}. He also served as {{ill|Governor of Harima|ja|播磨守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Harima|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 播磨国|1=Harima Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1047523}}.
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (720), he was appointed {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Seii Taishōgun}} to suppress a rebellion by the {{ill|Emishi|en|Emishi|WD=Q932574|lt=Emishi}}, following the assassination of {{ill|Kamu Yamakano|ja|上毛野広人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamu Yamakano|1=Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q45828852}}, the Inspector of Mutsu Province.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45–46]</ref> His campaign achieved partial success, and he was promoted to {{ill|Jūshi-i|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} upon his return.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 44]</ref>
=== Role in Court Politics ===
In {{ill|Shinki 6|ja|神亀6年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinki 6|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (729), Agatamori was appointed as a provisional {{ill|Sangi (Japan)|en|Sangi (Japan)|WD=Q1133663|lt=Sangi}} during the {{ill|Conspiracy of Prince Nagaya|ja|長屋王の変|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Conspiracy of Prince Nagaya|1=Prince Nagaya incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109357375}} and became a court noble shortly afterward, holding key positions under the {{ill|Fujiwara Four Brothers|ja|藤原四兄弟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Four Brothers|qq=}} regime. He reached the Rank of {{ill|Shōsan-i|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōsan-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} and served as {{ill|Chūnagon|en|Chūnagon|WD=Q1185382|lt=Chūnagon}}.
In {{ill|Tenpyō 7|ja|天平7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 7|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (735), he rejected a delegation from {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla}} that had unilaterally changed its national name.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Tenpyō 7, 2/27</ref>
Agatamori died in {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (737) at age 70, likely due to the smallpox epidemic that claimed many lives, including the Fujiwara brothers.<ref name="naoki" />
== Family ==
* Father: {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Shima|qq=}}<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Tenpyō 9, 6/23</ref> * Children: ** Daughter: Wife of {{ill|Fujiwara no Hamanari|en|Fujiwara no Hamanari|WD=Q3090622|lt=Fujiwara no Hamanari}}<ref>‘Sonpi Bunmyaku’</ref> ** Daughter: Possibly {{ill|Tanbe no Iratsume|ja|丹比郎女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanbe no Iratsume|1=Tambi no Iratsume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368537}}, wife of {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|en|Ōtomo no Tabito|WD=Q386287|lt=Ōtomo no Tabito}} and mother of {{ill|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|en|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|WD=Q386308|lt=Ōtomo no Yakamochi}}<ref>Kawakami [1971]</ref>

ISBN 978-4794215444. * Suzuki, Takuya. *{{ill|Emishi to Tōhoku Sensō|ja|蝦夷と東北戦争|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi to Tōhoku Sensō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*. Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2008. ISBN 978-4642063135. * Naoki, Kōjirō. *Nihon Kodai Chūsei Jinmei Jiten*. Yoshikawa Kōbu

Agatamori was later appointed {{ill|Governor of Musashi|ja|武蔵守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Musashi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武蔵国司|1=musashikokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546337}} and subsequently became one of the first {{ill|Inspectors (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inspectors (Ancient Japan)|1=Provincial Judge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q848663}}, overseeing the provinces of {{ill|Sagami|en|Sagami|WD=Q524473|lt=Sagami}}, {{ill|Ueno|en|Ueno|WD=Q576781|lt=Kōzuke}}, and {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke|1=Shimotsuke Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1208689}}. He also served as {{ill|Governor of Harima|ja|播磨守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Harima|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 播磨国|1=Harima Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1047523}}.
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (720), he was appointed {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Seii Taishōgun}} to suppress a rebellion by the {{ill|Emishi|en|Emishi|WD=Q932574|lt=Emishi}}, following the assassination of {{ill|Kamu Yamakano|ja|上毛野広人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamu Yamakano|1=Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q45828852}}, the Inspector of Mutsu Province.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45–46]</ref> His campaign achieved partial success, and he was promoted to {{ill|Jūshi-i|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} upon his return.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 44]</ref>
=== Role in Court Politics ===
In {{ill|Shinki 6|ja|神亀6年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinki 6|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (729), Agatamori was appointed as a provisional {{ill|Sangi (Japan)|en|Sangi (Japan)|WD=Q1133663|lt=Sangi}} during the {{ill|Conspiracy of Prince Nagaya|ja|長屋王の変|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Conspiracy of Prince Nagaya|1=Prince Nagaya incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109357375}} and became a court noble shortly afterward, holding key positions under the {{ill|Fujiwara Four Brothers|ja|藤原四兄弟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Four Brothers|qq=}} regime. He reached the Rank of {{ill|Shōsan-i|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōsan-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} and served as {{ill|Chūnagon|en|Chūnagon|WD=Q1185382|lt=Chūnagon}}.
In {{ill|Tenpyō 7|ja|天平7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 7|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (735), he rejected a delegation from {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla}} that had unilaterally changed its national name.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Tenpyō 7, 2/27</ref>
Agatamori died in {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (737) at age 70, likely due to the smallpox epidemic that claimed many lives, including the Fujiwara brothers.<ref name="naoki" />
== Family ==
* Father: {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Shima|qq=}}<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Tenpyō 9, 6/23</ref> * Children: ** Daughter: Wife of {{ill|Fujiwara no Hamanari|en|Fujiwara no Hamanari|WD=Q3090622|lt=Fujiwara no Hamanari}}<ref>‘Sonpi Bunmyaku’</ref> ** Daughter: Possibly {{ill|Tanbe no Iratsume|ja|丹比郎女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanbe no Iratsume|1=Tambi no Iratsume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368537}}, wife of {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|en|Ōtomo no Tabito|WD=Q386287|lt=Ōtomo no Tabito}} and mother of {{ill|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|en|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|WD=Q386308|lt=Ōtomo no Yakamochi}}<ref>Kawakami [1971]</ref>
== References ==
{{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography ==
* Ueda, Yū. *{{ill|遣唐使全航海|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/遣唐使全航海|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*. Sōshisha, 2006. ISBN 978-4794215444. * Suzuki, Takuya. *{{ill|Emishi to Tōhoku Sensō|ja|蝦夷と東北戦争|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi to Tōhoku Sensō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*. Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2008. ISBN 978-4642063135. * Naoki, Kōjirō. *Nihon Kodai Chūsei Jinmei Jiten*. Yoshikawa Kōbu

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fa:تاجیهی نو آگاتاموری]][[ja:多治比縣守]][[uk:Тадзіхі но Аґатаморі]][[zh:多治比县守]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fa:تاجیهی نو آگاتاموری]][[ja:多治比縣守]][[uk:Тадзіхі но Аґатаморі]][[zh:多治比县守]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fa:تاجیهی نو آگاتاموری]][[ja:多治比縣守]][[uk:Тадзіхі но Аґатаморі]][[zh:多治比县守]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0668|death_date={{death date|0737|7|25}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Q106191660|WD=Q106191660}}; {{ill|Q11368537|WD=Q11368537}}; {{ill|Q18048350|WD=Q18048350}}|father={{ill|Tajihi no Shima|WD=Q11430835}}}} '''Tajihi no Agatamori''' (多治比縣守) was a court noble and government official during the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}}. He was a son of {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Shima|qq=}}, who served as a {{ill|Minister of the Left|en|Minister of the Left|WD=Q949008|lt=Minister of the Left}} (Minister of the Left). Agatamori played key roles in diplomacy, governance, and military affairs, including serving as {{ill|Envoy to Tang China|ja|遣唐使押使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Envoy to Tang China|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Governor of Musashi|ja|武蔵守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Musashi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武蔵国司|1=musashikokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546337}}, and {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Seii Taishōgun}}.
== Biography ==
Agatamori was born in the 7th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji}}'s reign (668), as recorded in sources like the {{ill|Kugyō Bōnin|ja|Kugyō補任|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō Bōnin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which states he passed away in {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (737) at the age of 70.<ref name="naoki">Naoki [2006: 617]</ref> His family, the {{ill|Tajihi clan|en|Tajihi clan|WD=Q11430839|lt=Tajihi clan}}, descended from {{ill|Emperor Senka|en|Emperor Senka|WD=Q329728|lt=Emperor Senka}}, making them one of the prominent noble lineages of the time.<ref>Takashima [1969: 10–11]</ref>
=== Mission to Tang China ===
In {{ill|Keiun 2|ja|慶雲2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiun 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (705), Agatamori was promoted from {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=}} to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. His career advanced rapidly under the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|en|Empress Genmei|WD=Q233224|lt=Empress Genmei}}, and by {{ill|Wadō 8|ja|和銅8年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 8|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (715), he had reached {{ill|Jūshi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} and was appointed {{ill|Zōkyūkyō|ja|造宮卿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōkyūkyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造宮省|1=Zōgūshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11639741}}, overseeing the construction of {{ill|Heijō Palace|en|Heijō Palace|WD=Q211585|lt=Heijō Palace}}.
In {{ill|Reiki 2|ja|霊亀2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (716), Agatamori was appointed Envoy to Tang China (遣唐押使). He departed for China in {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (717), leading a large delegation of 557 members, including notable figures such as {{ill|Abe no Nakamaro|en|Abe no Nakamaro|WD=Q479788|lt=Abe no Nakamaro}}, {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|en|Kibi no Makibi|WD=Q1063276|lt=Kibi no Makibi}}, and {{ill|Genbō|en|Genbō|WD=Q657737|lt=Gembō}}. The delegation reached {{ill|Chang'an|en|Chang'an|WD=Q6501000|lt=Chang'an}} in October and returned safely in {{ill|Yōrō 2|ja|養老2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (718). Agatamori received further promotions upon his return.<ref>Ueda [2006: 80–83]</ref>
=== Governor and Regional Commander ===
Agatamori was later appointed {{ill|Governor of Musashi|ja|武蔵守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Musashi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武蔵国司|1=musashikokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546337}} and subsequently became one of the first {{ill|Inspectors (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inspectors (Ancient Japan)|1=Provincial Judge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q848663}}, overseeing the provinces of {{ill|Sagami|en|Sagami|WD=Q524473|lt=Sagami}}, {{ill|Ueno|en|Ueno|WD=Q576781|lt=Kōzuke}}, and {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke|1=Shimotsuke Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1208689}}. He also served as {{ill|Governor of Harima|ja|播磨守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Harima|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 播磨国|1=Harima Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1047523}}.
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (720), he was appointed {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Seii Taishōgun}} to suppress a rebellion by the {{ill|Emishi|en|Emishi|WD=Q932574|lt=Emishi}}, following the assassination of {{ill|Kamu Yamakano|ja|上毛野広人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamu Yamakano|1=Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q45828852}}, the Inspector of Mutsu Province.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45–46]</ref> His campaign achieved partial success, and he was promoted to {{ill|Jūshi-i|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} upon his return.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 44]</ref>
=== Role in Court Politics ===
In {{ill|Shinki 6|ja|神亀6年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinki 6|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (729), Agatamori was appointed as a provisional {{ill|Sangi (Japan)|en|Sangi (Japan)|WD=Q1133663|lt=Sangi}} during the {{ill|Conspiracy of Prince Nagaya|ja|長屋王の変|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Conspiracy of Prince Nagaya|1=Prince Nagaya incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109357375}} and became a court noble shortly afterward, holding key positions under the {{ill|Fujiwara Four Brothers|ja|藤原四兄弟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Four Brothers|qq=}} regime. He reached the Rank of {{ill|Shōsan-i|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōsan-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} and served as {{ill|Chūnagon|en|Chūnagon|WD=Q1185382|lt=Chūnagon}}.
In {{ill|Tenpyō 7|ja|天平7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 7|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (735), he rejected a delegation from {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla}} that had unilaterally changed its national name.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Tenpyō 7, 2/27</ref>
Agatamori died in {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (737) at age 70, likely due to the smallpox epidemic that claimed many lives, including the Fujiwara brothers.<ref name="naoki" />
== Family ==
* Father: {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Shima|qq=}}<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Tenpyō 9, 6/23</ref> * Children: ** Daughter: Wife of {{ill|Fujiwara no Hamanari|en|Fujiwara no Hamanari|WD=Q3090622|lt=Fujiwara no Hamanari}}<ref>‘Sonpi Bunmyaku’</ref> ** Daughter: Possibly {{ill|Tanbe no Iratsume|ja|丹比郎女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanbe no Iratsume|1=Tambi no Iratsume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368537}}, wife of {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|en|Ōtomo no Tabito|WD=Q386287|lt=Ōtomo no Tabito}} and mother of {{ill|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|en|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|WD=Q386308|lt=Ōtomo no Yakamochi}}<ref>Kawakami [1971]</ref>
== References ==
{{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography ==
* Ueda, Yū. *{{ill|遣唐使全航海|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/遣唐使全航海|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*. Sōshisha, 2006. ISBN 978-4794215444. * Suzuki, Takuya. *{{ill|Emishi to Tōhoku Sensō|ja|蝦夷と東北戦争|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi to Tōhoku Sensō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*. Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2008. ISBN 978-4642063135. * Naoki, Kōjirō. *Nihon Kodai Chūsei Jinmei Jiten*. Yoshikawa Kōbu

Agatamori was later appointed {{ill|Governor of Musashi|ja|武蔵守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Musashi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武蔵国司|1=musashikokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546337}} and subsequently became one of the first {{ill|Inspectors (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inspectors (Ancient Japan)|1=Provincial Judge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q848663}}, overseeing the provinces of {{ill|Sagami|en|Sagami|WD=Q524473|lt=Sagami}}, {{ill|Ueno|en|Ueno|WD=Q576781|lt=Kōzuke}}, and {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke|1=Shimotsuke Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1208689}}. He also served as {{ill|Governor of Harima|ja|播磨守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Harima|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 播磨国|1=Harima Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1047523}}.
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (720), he was appointed {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Seii Taishōgun}} to suppress a rebellion by the {{ill|Emishi|en|Emishi|WD=Q932574|lt=Emishi}}, following the assassination of {{ill|Kamu Yamakano|ja|上毛野広人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamu Yamakano|1=Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q45828852}}, the Inspector of Mutsu Province.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45–46]</ref> His campaign achieved partial success, and he was promoted to {{ill|Jūshi-i|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} upon his return.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 44]</ref>
=== Role in Court Politics ===
In {{ill|Shinki 6|ja|神亀6年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinki 6|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (729), Agatamori was appointed as a provisional {{ill|Sangi (Japan)|en|Sangi (Japan)|WD=Q1133663|lt=Sangi}} during the {{ill|Conspiracy of Prince Nagaya|ja|長屋王の変|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Conspiracy of Prince Nagaya|1=Prince Nagaya incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109357375}} and became a court noble shortly afterward, holding key positions under the {{ill|Fujiwara Four Brothers|ja|藤原四兄弟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Four Brothers|qq=}} regime. He reached the Rank of {{ill|Shōsan-i|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōsan-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} and served as {{ill|Chūnagon|en|Chūnagon|WD=Q1185382|lt=Chūnagon}}.
In {{ill|Tenpyō 7|ja|天平7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 7|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (735), he rejected a delegation from {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla}} that had unilaterally changed its national name.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Tenpyō 7, 2/27</ref>
Agatamori died in {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (737) at age 70, likely due to the smallpox epidemic that claimed many lives, including the Fujiwara brothers.<ref name="naoki" />
== Family ==
* Father: {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Shima|qq=}}<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Tenpyō 9, 6/23</ref> * Children: ** Daughter: Wife of {{ill|Fujiwara no Hamanari|en|Fujiwara no Hamanari|WD=Q3090622|lt=Fujiwara no Hamanari}}<ref>‘Sonpi Bunmyaku’</ref> ** Daughter: Possibly {{ill|Tanbe no Iratsume|ja|丹比郎女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanbe no Iratsume|1=Tambi no Iratsume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368537}}, wife of {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|en|Ōtomo no Tabito|WD=Q386287|lt=Ōtomo no Tabito}} and mother of {{ill|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|en|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|WD=Q386308|lt=Ōtomo no Yakamochi}}<ref>Kawakami [1971]</ref>

{{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography ==

ISBN 978-4794215444. * Suzuki, Takuya. *{{ill|Emishi to Tōhoku Sensō|ja|蝦夷と東北戦争|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi to Tōhoku Sensō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*. Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2008. ISBN 978-4642063135. * Naoki, Kōjirō. *Nihon Kodai Chūsei Jinmei Jiten*. Yoshikawa Kōbu

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fa:تاجیهی نو آگاتاموری]][[ja:多治比縣守]][[uk:Тадзіхі но Аґатаморі]][[zh:多治比县守]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fa:تاجیهی نو آگاتاموری]][[ja:多治比縣守]][[uk:Тадзіхі но Аґатаморі]][[zh:多治比县守]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fa:تاجیهی نو آگاتاموری]][[ja:多治比縣守]][[uk:Тадзіхі но Аґатаморі]][[zh:多治比县守]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0668|death_date={{death date|0737|7|25}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|children={{ill|Q106191660|WD=Q106191660|1=Daughter of Tajihi no Agatamori}}; {{ill|Q11368537|WD=Q11368537|1=Tambi no Iratsume}}; {{ill|Q18048350|WD=Q18048350|1=Tajihi no Kunibito}}|father={{ill|Tajihi no Shima|WD=Q11430835|1=Tajihi no Shima}}}} '''Tajihi no Agatamori''' (多治比縣守) was a court noble and government official during the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}. He was a son of {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Shima|qq=}}, who served as a {{ill|Minister of the Left|en|Minister of the Left|WD=Q949008|lt=Minister of the Left|1=Minister of the Left}} (Minister of the Left). Agatamori played key roles in diplomacy, governance, and military affairs, including serving as {{ill|Envoy to Tang China|ja|遣唐使押使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Envoy to Tang China|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Governor of Musashi|ja|武蔵守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Musashi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武蔵国司|1=musashikokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546337}}, and {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Seii Taishōgun|1=Shogun}}.
== Biography ==
Agatamori was born in the 7th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji|1=Emperor Tenji}}'s reign (668), as recorded in sources like the {{ill|Kugyō Bōnin|ja|Kugyō補任|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō Bōnin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which states he passed away in {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (737) at the age of 70.<ref name="naoki">Naoki [2006: 617]</ref> His family, the {{ill|Tajihi clan|en|Tajihi clan|WD=Q11430839|lt=Tajihi clan|1=Tajihi clan}}, descended from {{ill|Emperor Senka|en|Emperor Senka|WD=Q329728|lt=Emperor Senka|1=Emperor Senka}}, making them one of the prominent noble lineages of the time.<ref>Takashima [1969: 10–11]</ref>
=== Mission to Tang China ===
In {{ill|Keiun 2|ja|慶雲2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiun 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (705), Agatamori was promoted from {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=}} to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. His career advanced rapidly under the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|en|Empress Genmei|WD=Q233224|lt=Empress Genmei|1=Empress Genmei}}, and by {{ill|Wadō 8|ja|和銅8年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 8|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (715), he had reached {{ill|Jūshi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} and was appointed {{ill|Zōkyūkyō|ja|造宮卿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōkyūkyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造宮省|1=Zōgūshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11639741}}, overseeing the construction of {{ill|Heijō Palace|en|Heijō Palace|WD=Q211585|lt=Heijō Palace|1=Heijō Palace}}.
In {{ill|Reiki 2|ja|霊亀2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (716), Agatamori was appointed Envoy to Tang China (遣唐押使). He departed for China in {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (717), leading a large delegation of 557 members, including notable figures such as {{ill|Abe no Nakamaro|en|Abe no Nakamaro|WD=Q479788|lt=Abe no Nakamaro|1=Abe no Nakamaro}}, {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|en|Kibi no Makibi|WD=Q1063276|lt=Kibi no Makibi|1=Kibi no Makibi}}, and {{ill|Genbō|en|Genbō|WD=Q657737|lt=Gembō|1=Genbō}}. The delegation reached {{ill|Chang'an|en|Chang'an|WD=Q6501000|lt=Chang'an|1=Chang'an}} in October and returned safely in {{ill|Yōrō 2|ja|養老2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (718). Agatamori received further promotions upon his return.<ref>Ueda [2006: 80–83]</ref>
=== Governor and Regional Commander ===
Agatamori was later appointed {{ill|Governor of Musashi|ja|武蔵守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Musashi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武蔵国司|1=musashikokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546337}} and subsequently became one of the first {{ill|Inspectors (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inspectors (Ancient Japan)|1=Provincial Judge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q848663}}, overseeing the provinces of {{ill|Sagami|en|Sagami|WD=Q524473|lt=Sagami|1=Sagami}}, {{ill|Ueno|en|Ueno|WD=Q576781|lt=Kōzuke|1=Ueno}}, and {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke|1=下野|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1208689}}. He also served as {{ill|Governor of Harima|ja|播磨守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Harima|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 播磨国|1=Harima Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1047523}}.
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (720), he was appointed {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Seii Taishōgun|1=Shogun}} to suppress a rebellion by the {{ill|Emishi|en|Emishi|WD=Q932574|lt=Emishi|1=Emishi}}, following the assassination of {{ill|Kamu Yamakano|ja|上毛野広人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamu Yamakano|1=Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q45828852}}, the Inspector of Mutsu Province.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45–46]</ref> His campaign achieved partial success, and he was promoted to {{ill|Jūshi-i|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} upon his return.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 44]</ref>
=== Role in Court Politics ===
In {{ill|Shinki 6|ja|神亀6年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinki 6|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (729), Agatamori was appointed as a provisional {{ill|Sangi (Japan)|en|Sangi (Japan)|WD=Q1133663|lt=Sangi|1=Sangi (Japan)}} during the {{ill|Conspiracy of Prince Nagaya|ja|長屋王の変|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Conspiracy of Prince Nagaya|1=Prince Nagaya incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109357375}} and became a court noble shortly afterward, holding key positions under the {{ill|Fujiwara Four Brothers|ja|藤原四兄弟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Four Brothers|qq=}} regime. He reached the Rank of {{ill|Shōsan-i|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōsan-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} and served as {{ill|Chūnagon|en|Chūnagon|WD=Q1185382|lt=Chūnagon|1=Chūnagon}}.
In {{ill|Tenpyō 7|ja|天平7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 7|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (735), he rejected a delegation from {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla|1=Silla}} that had unilaterally changed its national name.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Tenpyō 7, 2/27</ref>
Agatamori died in {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (737) at age 70, likely due to the smallpox epidemic that claimed many lives, including the Fujiwara brothers.<ref name="naoki" />
== Family ==
* Father: {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Shima|qq=}}<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Tenpyō 9, 6/23</ref> * Children: ** Daughter: Wife of {{ill|Fujiwara no Hamanari|en|Fujiwara no Hamanari|WD=Q3090622|lt=Fujiwara no Hamanari|1=Fujiwara no Hamanari}}<ref>‘Sonpi Bunmyaku’</ref> ** Daughter: Possibly {{ill|Tanbe no Iratsume|ja|丹比郎女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanbe no Iratsume|1=Tambi no Iratsume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368537}}, wife of {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|en|Ōtomo no Tabito|WD=Q386287|lt=Ōtomo no Tabito|1=Ōtomo no Tabito}} and mother of {{ill|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|en|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|WD=Q386308|lt=Ōtomo no Yakamochi|1=Ōtomo no Yakamochi}}<ref>Kawakami [1971]</ref>

== Bibliography ==

ISBN 978-4794215444. * Suzuki, Takuya. *{{ill|Emishi to Tōhoku Sensō|ja|蝦夷と東北戦争|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi to Tōhoku Sensō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*. Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2008. ISBN 978-4642063135. * Naoki, Kōjirō. *Nihon Kodai Chūsei Jinmei Jiten*. Yoshikawa Kōbu

=== Governor and Regional Commander ===
Agatamori was later appointed {{ill|Governor of Musashi|ja|武蔵守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Musashi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武蔵国司|1=musashikokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546337}} and subsequently became one of the first {{ill|Inspectors (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inspectors (Ancient Japan)|1=Provincial Judge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q848663}}, overseeing the provinces of {{ill|Sagami|en|Sagami|WD=Q524473|lt=Sagami|1=Sagami}}, {{ill|Ueno|en|Ueno|WD=Q576781|lt=Kōzuke|1=Ueno}}, and {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke|1=下野|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1208689}}. He also served as {{ill|Governor of Harima|ja|播磨守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Harima|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 播磨国|1=Harima Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1047523}}.
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (720), he was appointed {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Seii Taishōgun|1=Shogun}} to suppress a rebellion by the {{ill|Emishi|en|Emishi|WD=Q932574|lt=Emishi|1=Emishi}}, following the assassination of {{ill|Kamu Yamakano|ja|上毛野広人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamu Yamakano|1=Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q45828852}}, the Inspector of Mutsu Province.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45–46]</ref> His campaign achieved partial success, and he was promoted to {{ill|Jūshi-i|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} upon his return.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 44]</ref>
=== Role in Court Politics ===
In {{ill|Shinki 6|ja|神亀6年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinki 6|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (729), Agatamori was appointed as a provisional {{ill|Sangi (Japan)|en|Sangi (Japan)|WD=Q1133663|lt=Sangi|1=Sangi (Japan)}} during the {{ill|Conspiracy of Prince Nagaya|ja|長屋王の変|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Conspiracy of Prince Nagaya|1=Prince Nagaya incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109357375}} and became a court noble shortly afterward, holding key positions under the {{ill|Fujiwara Four Brothers|ja|藤原四兄弟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Four Brothers|qq=}} regime. He reached the Rank of {{ill|Shōsan-i|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōsan-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} and served as {{ill|Chūnagon|en|Chūnagon|WD=Q1185382|lt=Chūnagon|1=Chūnagon}}.
In {{ill|Tenpyō 7|ja|天平7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 7|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (735), he rejected a delegation from {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla|1=Silla}} that had unilaterally changed its national name.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Tenpyō 7, 2/27</ref>
Agatamori died in {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (737) at age 70, likely due to the smallpox epidemic that claimed many lives, including the Fujiwara brothers.<ref name="naoki" />
== Family ==
* Father: {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Shima|qq=}}<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Tenpyō 9, 6/23</ref> * Children: ** Daughter: Wife of {{ill|Fujiwara no Hamanari|en|Fujiwara no Hamanari|WD=Q3090622|lt=Fujiwara no Hamanari|1=Fujiwara no Hamanari}}<ref>‘Sonpi Bunmyaku’</ref> ** Daughter: Possibly {{ill|Tanbe no Iratsume|ja|丹比郎女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanbe no Iratsume|1=Tambi no Iratsume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368537}}, wife of {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|en|Ōtomo no Tabito|WD=Q386287|lt=Ōtomo no Tabito|1=Ōtomo no Tabito}} and mother of {{ill|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|en|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|WD=Q386308|lt=Ōtomo no Yakamochi|1=Ōtomo no Yakamochi}}<ref>Kawakami [1971]</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fa:تاجیهی نو آگاتاموری]][[ja:多治比縣守]][[uk:Тадзіхі но Аґатаморі]][[zh:多治比县守]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fa:تاجیهی نو آگاتاموری]][[ja:多治比縣守]][[uk:Тадзіхі но Аґатаморі]][[zh:多治比县守]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fa:تاجیهی نو آگاتاموری]][[ja:多治比縣守]][[uk:Тадзіхі но Аґатаморі]][[zh:多治比县守]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0668|death_date={{death date|0737|7|25}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|children={{ill|Q106191660|WD=Q106191660|1=Daughter of Tajihi no Agatamori}}; {{ill|Q11368537|WD=Q11368537|1=Tambi no Iratsume}}; {{ill|Q18048350|WD=Q18048350|1=Tajihi no Kunibito}}|father={{ill|Tajihi no Shima|WD=Q11430835|1=Tajihi no Shima}}}} '''Tajihi no Agatamori''' (多治比縣守) was a court noble and government official during the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}. He was a son of {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Shima|qq=}}, who served as a {{ill|Minister of the Left|en|Minister of the Left|WD=Q949008|lt=Minister of the Left|1=Minister of the Left}} (Minister of the Left). Agatamori played key roles in diplomacy, governance, and military affairs, including serving as {{ill|Envoy to Tang China|ja|遣唐使押使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Envoy to Tang China|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Governor of Musashi|ja|武蔵守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Musashi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武蔵国司|1=musashikokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546337}}, and {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Seii Taishōgun|1=Shogun}}.
== Biography ==
Agatamori was born in the 7th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji|1=Emperor Tenji}}'s reign (668), as recorded in sources like the {{ill|Kugyō Bōnin|ja|Kugyō補任|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō Bōnin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which states he passed away in {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (737) at the age of 70.<ref name="naoki">Naoki [2006: 617]</ref> His family, the {{ill|Tajihi clan|en|Tajihi clan|WD=Q11430839|lt=Tajihi clan|1=Tajihi clan}}, descended from {{ill|Emperor Senka|en|Emperor Senka|WD=Q329728|lt=Emperor Senka|1=Emperor Senka}}, making them one of the prominent noble lineages of the time.<ref>Takashima [1969: 10–11]</ref>
=== Mission to Tang China ===
In {{ill|Keiun 2|ja|慶雲2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiun 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (705), Agatamori was promoted from {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=}} to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. His career advanced rapidly under the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|en|Empress Genmei|WD=Q233224|lt=Empress Genmei|1=Empress Genmei}}, and by {{ill|Wadō 8|ja|和銅8年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 8|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (715), he had reached {{ill|Jūshi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} and was appointed {{ill|Zōkyūkyō|ja|造宮卿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōkyūkyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造宮省|1=Zōgūshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11639741}}, overseeing the construction of {{ill|Heijō Palace|en|Heijō Palace|WD=Q211585|lt=Heijō Palace|1=Heijō Palace}}.
In {{ill|Reiki 2|ja|霊亀2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (716), Agatamori was appointed Envoy to Tang China (遣唐押使). He departed for China in {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (717), leading a large delegation of 557 members, including notable figures such as {{ill|Abe no Nakamaro|en|Abe no Nakamaro|WD=Q479788|lt=Abe no Nakamaro|1=Abe no Nakamaro}}, {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|en|Kibi no Makibi|WD=Q1063276|lt=Kibi no Makibi|1=Kibi no Makibi}}, and {{ill|Genbō|en|Genbō|WD=Q657737|lt=Gembō|1=Genbō}}. The delegation reached {{ill|Chang'an|en|Chang'an|WD=Q6501000|lt=Chang'an|1=Chang'an}} in October and returned safely in {{ill|Yōrō 2|ja|養老2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (718). Agatamori received further promotions upon his return.<ref>Ueda [2006: 80–83]</ref>
=== Governor and Regional Commander ===
Agatamori was later appointed {{ill|Governor of Musashi|ja|武蔵守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Musashi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武蔵国司|1=musashikokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546337}} and subsequently became one of the first {{ill|Inspectors (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inspectors (Ancient Japan)|1=Provincial Judge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q848663}}, overseeing the provinces of {{ill|Sagami|en|Sagami|WD=Q524473|lt=Sagami|1=Sagami}}, {{ill|Ueno|en|Ueno|WD=Q576781|lt=Kōzuke|1=Ueno}}, and {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke|1=下野|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1208689}}. He also served as {{ill|Governor of Harima|ja|播磨守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Harima|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 播磨国|1=Harima Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1047523}}.
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (720), he was appointed {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Seii Taishōgun|1=Shogun}} to suppress a rebellion by the {{ill|Emishi|en|Emishi|WD=Q932574|lt=Emishi|1=Emishi}}, following the assassination of {{ill|Kamu Yamakano|ja|上毛野広人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamu Yamakano|1=Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q45828852}}, the Inspector of Mutsu Province.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45–46]</ref> His campaign achieved partial success, and he was promoted to {{ill|Jūshi-i|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} upon his return.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 44]</ref>
=== Role in Court Politics ===
In {{ill|Shinki 6|ja|神亀6年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinki 6|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (729), Agatamori was appointed as a provisional {{ill|Sangi (Japan)|en|Sangi (Japan)|WD=Q1133663|lt=Sangi|1=Sangi (Japan)}} during the {{ill|Conspiracy of Prince Nagaya|ja|長屋王の変|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Conspiracy of Prince Nagaya|1=Prince Nagaya incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109357375}} and became a court noble shortly afterward, holding key positions under the {{ill|Fujiwara Four Brothers|ja|藤原四兄弟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Four Brothers|qq=}} regime. He reached the Rank of {{ill|Shōsan-i|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōsan-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} and served as {{ill|Chūnagon|en|Chūnagon|WD=Q1185382|lt=Chūnagon|1=Chūnagon}}.
In {{ill|Tenpyō 7|ja|天平7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 7|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (735), he rejected a delegation from {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla|1=Silla}} that had unilaterally changed its national name.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Tenpyō 7, 2/27</ref>
Agatamori died in {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (737) at age 70, likely due to the smallpox epidemic that claimed many lives, including the Fujiwara brothers.<ref name="naoki" />
